Sign in to load your franchise ledger, or create a new account.

Cloud saves are tied to the account you log into.

ℹ Contracts Screen Guide

Action row: ▼ down · ▲ up · ⇡ move to top · 🕒 defer to back burner · ✂ split · ⚭ merge · 📌 pin · 🗂 archive · 💰 claim gold.

Split: break a task with 2+ sub-tasks into two tasks. Both halves stay in the same bordered panel.

Merge: combine two tasks into one master. Choose the new title and the order of the combined sub-tasks.

Pin (📌): mirrors any task or sub-task at the top of the screen for prominence. Toggling progress on the pinned copy updates the original — they share the same data.

Favourites & scheduled contracts: weekly / monthly / yearly tasks scheduled in The Archive auto-activate at your configured start-of-day.

✦ Contract Archive

⭐ = favourite (daily/custom auto-activates at morning reset). Weekly, monthly, and yearly tasks can be scheduled with Pick selectors and move to Contracts at the configured start of day. ↑↓ reorder within group. Action icons: ⇡ move to top, 🪙 claim gold, ▶ activate, 🗑 delete.

✦ The Adventuring Party
✦ Recruitment Office
Review candidates, hirelings, or cancel to return to the Party screen.
Standard Recruitment

Choose from five candidates. First roll is free — additional rerolls cost 10 gp.

Roll the dice to reveal candidates…
🪓 Hire a Hireling

Low-cost labour with minimal abilities. Cannot go on quests alone. First roll is free — rerolls cost 5 gp.

Roll to find available labour…
⭐ Custom Mercenary

Hand-pick attributes for a higher fee. Each selected attribute adds 50 gp to the base cost. Skills capped at 4, tools at 2, languages at 2 — same as standard, but you choose which ones. Comes with a class-appropriate equipment kit.

You may pick up to 4 skill pills. Leave all unselected to randomize class-appropriate skills.
You may pick up to 2 tool pills. Leave all unselected to randomize tools.
You may pick up to 2 language pills. Common is always included automatically.
🪙 500 gp base
Base 500 gp + 0 gp in options = 500 gp total

✦ The Wandering Emporium
★ → 💰 Convert QP to Gold
Rate: 5 QP = 1 gp · Balance: 0 QP

🎟 Class Codes

Generate login codes for students. Each code creates an independent save slot — no email or personal data required.

Relentless Quest
Relentless Quest
Version 1.6942
Dev mode: navy quick-access bar below the header with Ledger, Content Editor, version number, and Exit Dev Mode.
Branding
Daily Reset
Use an exact time. Favourite contracts auto-activate once each day the first time the app reaches or passes that time.
📜 Change Log
Tap to review notable stable updates.
Version 1.6942 — Crash fix + post-hire character modal + clickable unassigned chip. (1) Crash fix: Manage Squads button was non-functional. squadTerritory() was iterating cycleSquadMap() with for...in and unconditionally calling .trim() on every value, but the special GRIND_CYCLE_KEY ('__grind__') slot holds an array of squad names rather than a string (v1.6910's multi-squad grind clock-in). Calling .trim() on the array threw TypeError: (map[setId] || "").trim is not a function, which blew up the entire Squad Manager open chain (openSquadManager → renderSquadManager → renderSqmgrGrid → the squad-tile .map() that calls squadTerritory). Fix: squadTerritory now continues past GRIND_CYCLE_KEY and defensively skips any non-string value. Grind squads have no cycle-territory concept anyway — they aren't tied to a QUESTSETS entry with a territory field. (2) Post-hire character modal. Both hire paths (standard recruiter and custom mercenary) now open the character peek modal (ov-charpeek) when the player taps the "View" button in the post-hire celebration overlay (hire-modal-view). Previously: standard hires scrolled to a party row that no longer exists as a standalone card (v1.6898 removed per-char cards; everyone renders inside squad panels now), so "View" effectively did nothing visible; mercenary hires skipped the celebration entirely and just scrolled the page. Both now call openCharacterPeekByName(charName) after a 120ms delay (enough for navTo('party')'s layout settle). Mercenary hire also now surfaces the celebration ribbon (showHireRibbon) with the same 2-button overlay as standard hires — consistency win. (3) Clickable "unassigned" chip. In the character peek modal header subtitle, when a character is in zero squads, the "unassigned" label now renders as a teal dashed-border pill (🧭 unassigned) with data-a="charpeek-assign". Tapping opens a new ov-assign-squad modal listing every non-cycle-locked squad with member count + capacity, plus a "➕ Create new squad" fallback. Selecting a squad routes through sqmgrAssignChar(ci, squadName) so all existing checks apply (territory locks, capacity cap of 10, already-busy character detection). Full squads render disabled at 50% opacity. Post-assignment, the charpeek header re-renders so the new squad name shows immediately. Non-assigned characters still show the "unassigned" chip; re-clicking reopens the picker. (4) New assets: ov-assign-squad modal HTML (z:510 so it stacks above ov-charpeek at z:500, same pattern as ov-cpk-inv); openAssignSquadModal(ci) function; charpeek-assign click delegation; subtitle rendering migrated from .textContent to .innerHTML (all inserted values go through esc()). No SQL change. No data-model change.
Version 1.6941 — Per-squad shopping list integrating Quest Preview with Squad-Manager Focus Mode. Four coordinated changes. (1) Quest Preview asks "scouting for which squad?" up front. openQuestPreviewList now gates rendering behind a Step 0 squad picker. Each squad renders as a colored chip showing member count, 🔒 cycle-lock, and a 🛒 badge with the squad's current shopping-list size. Once chosen, the selection persists in a module-scoped _qpvSquad var for the session (clears when the named squad is later deleted or renamed). A "Change squad" link on the squad banner reopens the picker at any time. (2) Per-quest coverage pills on the list view. Every quest row now shows a pill next to its title indicating how this squad fares against its attribute requirements: forest "✓ fully covered" when all skill/tool/lang/faith reqs are met; crimson "N/M covered" when partially covered; neutral "no attr reqs" when the quest has no character-attribute requirements at all. Item and gold prereqs are intentionally excluded from the count since they aren't addressable by recruiting. (3) Per-requirement +/✓ buttons on the detail view. The Prerequisites section of openQuestPreview now renders each skill/tool/language/faith as its own row with an inline status button: teal "✓ {squad name}" if the squad covers it; gold "🛒 on list" (tappable to remove) if it's already on the shopping list; crimson "+ list" button if it's uncovered and not yet listed (tappable to add). Items and gold reqs still render plain-text (no button) since they can't be recruited for. A header strip above the prereq block summarizes overall coverage. (4) 🛒 Shopping button in Squad Manager toolbar. Fourth button added to the sqmgr toolbar (same row as 🎯 Fill Role / 🔍 Overlap / ⚖ Rebalance). Label shows total count across all squads — "🛒 Shopping (7)" — and disables entirely when no squad has items. Clicking: if exactly one squad has items, auto-loads it; if multiple do, prompts with a numbered list. On load, enters Focus Mode with (a) the target squad preselected, (b) the first 3 shopping-list items auto-pushed into sqmgrFocusPicks, (c) the axis tab switched to the first pick's axis. A new gold banner appears at the top of the focus panel showing "Shopping list loaded · Showing items 1–3 of N · the picks below auto-filled from the list" with a ▶ Next 3 rotation button (only when N > 3) and a ✕ Exit button that drops back to normal Fill Role without affecting the stored list. Tapping Next 3 advances sqmgrShoppingOffset by 3 and wraps to zero at end-of-list. Changing squad mid-flow cancels shopping mode. Data model: S._shoppingList is a plain object mapping squad name → array of {axis, value, fromQuest, addedAt}. Rides inside the existing settings blob (same persistence path as S._sqmgrPins). If a squad is renamed, its list becomes orphaned — _openShoppingMode filters to only squads that still exist, and the list is intentionally tied to the name rather than a UUID because squads are user-named rather than id-referenced in most of the codebase. New helpers: _shoppingListFor, _isInShoppingList, _addToShoppingList, _removeFromShoppingList, _shoppingListCount, _shoppingListTotalCount, _qpvSquadCovers, _qpvCoverageSummary, _openShoppingMode, _applyShoppingPicks, sqmgrShoppingNextPage. New state vars: sqmgrShoppingMode, sqmgrShoppingOffset, _qpvSquad. No SQL change. No change to the 3-pick cap in normal Fill Role — the cap only relaxes in shopping mode via rotation, per the explicit design decision to avoid UI clutter.
Version 1.6940 — Contracts sub-task action drawer gains a third icon: ⇈ super-up, which moves the sub-task to the top (index 0) of its parent task's sub-task list in one tap. Previously the only way to promote a sub-task to the top of its list was to drag it manually past every item above it — workable for rearranging 2–3 rows, increasingly painful for a long task. Render: new button injected in .strow-actions drawer before the existing ⇄ move and 📌 pin buttons (reading order: super-up → move → pin). Glyph is ⇈ (U+21C8, upward double arrow), styled teal via new .sd-supup CSS variant. Auto-disables with disabled attribute + reduced opacity when the sub-task is already at index 0, so the button communicates "you can't go any higher" without a confirm-and-alert. Handler: new data-a="stsupup" branch next to stpin in the main click delegate. Reads data-i (task index) and data-si (sub-task index), validates bounds, splice(_supsi,1) to pull, unshift to push to position 0. No-op on already-top or out-of-range. Logs the move as a task-type entry with the sub-task name. Drawer widening: adding a third 44-min-width button required bumping .strow.strow-show-actions .strow-main{transform:translateX(...)} from -100px to -148px so the drawer reveals wide enough for all three buttons without clipping. The swipe threshold / horizontal-vs-vertical ratio checks are untouched — the extra 48px of reveal fits comfortably below the ratio check's horizontal-dominance test. No data model change, no SQL change, no new state.
Version 1.6939 — Squad-manager overlap highlighter redesigned around pair insignia. v1.6938's severity-tier approach was correct about lowering the threshold but wrong about the information density — every overlapping character's card carried a wordy partner block like "↔ Lindir (2 shared: Nature, Stealth)" which made even a modestly-overlapping squad look like a wall of text. New model: (1) Threshold tightened to 2+ shared attributes per pair (down from the v1.6938 threshold of 1 "any overlap counts"; up from the original v1.6936 threshold of 3). Two is the user's stated rule: "if two characters match on two or more facets, label them both." (2) Each qualifying pair gets a unique insignia assigned from a palette of eight geometric glyphs — 🟦 🟩 🟨 🟥 🟪 🟧 🟫 ⚫ — chosen so none collides with: the 16 squad emojis (⚔ 🛡 🗡 🏹 🔥 ❄ ⚡ 🌿 💀 🐺 🦅 🌙 ☀ ⭐ 🎯 🪄); the ribbon-label glyphs (🙏 🗣 🔧 ⭐); the pin toggles (📌 📍); the XP/star decorations (✨ 💫 🌟 ⭐); any warning/money/tool indicator used elsewhere. Both characters in the pair get the SAME insignia rendered inline next to their name, so a match reads instantly — see two 🟦 in the same squad? that's the pair. A character in multiple pairs collects multiple insignia in a single inline row. (3) Intra-squad adjacency sort: within each squad bucket, paired characters are re-ordered so partners sit as immediate siblings (stronger pairs — more shared attrs — anchor first). Cross-squad ordering and the existing sqmgrSortSquad preference are preserved; the adjacency pass only mutates positions inside a single squad's contiguous range. Non-paired members fall naturally after the pairs. (4) Tooltip-on-hover: each insignia has a title attribute with the partner's name and the specific shared attributes (e.g. "Feana — 2 shared: Nature, Stealth"), so the drill-down info the old partner block exposed is still one hover away without consuming any card real estate. (5) Removed: the partner text block, the crimson/amber/orange severity borders, the "⚠ overlap ×N" badge, and the severity-tier CSS (kept as unused back-compat classes in case any other code path tries to set them, but no longer emitted). Card borders stay neutral — the insignia and adjacency do all the signaling. New function shape: computeOverlapCiSet() now returns {byCi, pairOrder}: byCi[ci] is an array of {insignia, partnerCi, attrs, count} for rendering; pairOrder is a flat array of pair records with {ciA, ciB, insignia, squadName, count} for driving the sort. Two new CSS classes (.sqmgr-overlap-insignia-row, .sqmgr-overlap-insignia) do the inline layout with a subtle drop-shadow for legibility against both dark squad-color and parchment-color backgrounds. No SQL change, no new state vars, no QP changes.
Version 1.6938 — Bug fix + UX tightening for the squad-manager overlap highlighter. Root cause of the bug: v1.6937 claimed to ship two changes to overlap: (a) lower-noise rich return format surfacing partner names and shared attributes, and (b) the still-in-place severity-3 threshold. The rich render code was shipped (card partner block + "×N" badge + _overlapInfo.partners.map(...)) but the computeOverlapCiSet function was NOT updated to match — it was still returning the old boolean {ci:true} shape with shared as a scalar counter. Any time overlap was toggled on, _overlapInfo.partners evaluated to undefined on truthy entries, and the render either silently failed inside the .map() call or produced garbage HTML — which is why the user saw the crimson glow working for some pairs but never saw partner-name info, and why 1- and 2-attribute overlaps weren't flagged at all (the old threshold of 3 was still in effect). Fix: computeOverlapCiSet rewritten to match the consumer contract. Threshold lowered from 3 to 1 so any shared attribute within a squad now surfaces — reflects the real user need that two hirelings both doing Arcana, or two leveled chars both doing Nature + Stealth, represent wasted coverage regardless of how few attributes overlap. Return shape: {ci:{partners:[{ci,count,attrs:[...]}], maxCount:N, severity:'mild'|'moderate'|'severe'}}. Severity tiers: 1 shared attr → mild (amber 2px border, no glow, "⚠ overlap ×1" amber badge); 2 shared → moderate (orange border + faint glow, orange badge); 3+ shared → severe (crimson border + strong glow, crimson badge) — the original v1.6936 visual weight. The partner block inside each card also tints its background more heavily at higher severity (.10/.15/.22 alpha on crimson). Users can now see the worst offenders visually at a glance, while minor overlaps register without screaming. Why severity tiers instead of just filtering low-severity out: the user's feedback specifically said 1- and 2-attribute overlaps ARE interesting — they just shouldn't dominate the view. Tiering preserves the signal while making the hierarchy obvious. Back-compat: the legacy .overlap-warn CSS class is still in the stylesheet mapped to the severe visual treatment, in case any other code path sets it. No data migration, no SQL change, no new state vars; one function rewrite + three new CSS classes + two-line render tweak using the new .severity field.
Version 1.6937 — Three coordinated changes. (1) 📖 Quest Preview. New Quest Map button on every incomplete cycle card (teal, alongside Activate / Abandon) opens a read-only two-stage preview flow with no side effects: no cycle activation, no squad assignment, no QP spend, no commitment. Modal ov-quest-preview-list shows the cycle theme as a banner, then every quest in the set as a tappable row — quest title, type icon (⚔ combat / 🗺 explore / 💬 social), reward, min-level, type label, and a lightweight prereq summary (e.g. "2 skills · 1 tool · faith"). Done quests render with a ✓ and green border using the same done-state resolution as the Quest Log (active cycle's live qs.done if the set is active, otherwise setProgress[setId].done). Tapping a row opens the drill-down ov-quest-preview at z:510 showing full description, an "At a glance" grid (💰 reward · ⏱ duration via questDurationLabel · 📊 min level · 👥 squad cap from squadReqMax), and a full prereq breakdown across all six axes (skills, tools, languages, faith, items, gold). Back button returns to the list; Close dismisses both. In-progress quests on the active cycle are flagged with a ⏳ subtitle. Completed cycles intentionally don't get the Preview button because they already have 📜 Quest Log which conveys similar info with completion state. (2) Overlap highlighter now names its partners. Previously computeOverlapCiSet returned only {ci:true}, so you'd see the crimson glow + "⚠ overlap" badge but had no way to tell with whom a character overlapped — you'd have to manually cross-reference their squadmates' skill lists. Now it returns {ci:{partners:[{ci,count,attrs:[...]}], maxCount:N}}. Each overlapping character's card renders a crimson-tinted block inside the card body listing every partner by name with the exact shared attributes: "↔ Lindir (4 shared: Athletics, Insight, Perception, Thieves' Tools)" — truncated to first 4 attrs with "+N more" suffix if more exist. The warning badge updates from static "⚠ overlap" to "⚠ overlap ×N" where N is the max shared count across all that character's pairings, so the worst offenders sort visually. Badge moved from right to top-left so it doesn't collide with the pin toggle. (3) Skill filter ribbons temporarily hidden. The four ribbons under the squad-manager header (⭐ Skills / 🙏 Faith / 🗣 Lang / 🔧 Tools) are hidden via display:none on #sqmgr-ribbons. Rendering / wiring still runs (so re-enabling is one CSS value), but the strip no longer eats vertical space — Focus Mode's axis tabs already surface the same info contextually. To restore: remove display:none from the #sqmgr-ribbons inline style. sqmgrRibbonFilter is still cleared on modal open so no hidden filter can silently suppress characters. No SQL change for any of the three.
Version 1.6936 — Squad-manager redesign for mobile coverage analysis. Three coordinated features added to the ov-squad-mgr modal. (1) 🎯 Fill Role (Focus Mode): new toolbar button opens an inline panel. Step 1 — pick a target squad from a colored chip row (shows current coverage % per squad, 🔒 for cycle-locked). Step 2 — four axis tabs (⭐ Skills / 🔧 Tools / 🗣 Lang / 🙏 Faith) surface the attributes that squad is missing on the selected axis, plus an already-covered strip at reduced opacity. Tap up to 3 attributes to "pick" them; current picks render as dismissible gold chips. Step 3 — a ranked donor list populates automatically: every non-busy character in another squad or unassigned who covers at least one pick, ranked by match count (N/3), then by coverage delta, then by level. Each row shows a big match-count badge (filled teal for perfect N/N matches), source squad, matched attribute tags in teal highlight, and a ±% coverage delta pill. Tap a row to move that character into the target (with confirm dialog). Characters in cycle-locked squads show 🔒 and display a dialog redirecting to the Swap Zone. After a move, the panel re-renders so updated coverage + new donor rankings surface immediately. (2) 🔍 Overlap: toolbar toggle that scans every squad for pairs of members sharing 3 or more attributes combined across all 4 axes (skills + tools/weapons + languages + faith). Flagged characters get a crimson glow + "⚠ overlap" badge on their card. Makes it obvious when you have two Athletics-Insight-Perception-Thieves-tools characters redundantly stacked on the same squad. Toggle off to clear. (3) ⚖ Rebalance (QP spend): new QP sink that auto-distributes all unlocked squads into the highest-average-coverage arrangement found via hill-climbing swap search. Cost: 25 QP when no squad is cycle-locked; 50 QP when any squad is locked (the surcharge reflects the logistical nightmare of rearranging people around characters whose squads can't be touched). Button label live-updates with the current cost, disables when QP is insufficient or insufficient party, and shows a preview alert with the confirm. Optimizer excludes locked squads from the move pool entirely (their members stay put), respects the busy set (active quest/grind/downtime), and respects a new per-character 📌 pin toggle that renders in the top-right corner of every character card — pinned chars are locked in place for rebalance but can still be moved manually. Algorithm: iterates up to 40 passes, each pass evaluates (unassigned→squad adds, squad→unassigned removes, squad↔squad swaps), applies the single best op that improves average coverage %, repeats until no ε > 0.01 improvement found. If the result is no better than the starting arrangement, QP is not charged and the user is informed. On success: QP spent, move count + coverage delta logged to adventure log, party + squad manager re-render, and a result summary alert shows the before/after average. (4) Infrastructure: five new state vars (sqmgrFocusMode, sqmgrFocusSquad, sqmgrFocusAxis, sqmgrFocusPicks, sqmgrOverlapOn); one new persistent field (S._sqmgrPins, a map of ci → true); eight new helper functions (_charAttrsOnAxis, _charHasAttr, _squadMissingOnAxis, _squadCoveredOnAxis, _axisLabel, computeOverlapCiSet, computeDonorRankings, _rebalanceCost); three new render/control functions (renderSqmgrFocusPanel, updateSqmgrFocusToolbar, runAutoRebalance). Two new QP manager cost fields (costAutoRebalance, costAutoRebalanceLocked) with defaults and load/save plumbing; both appear in the QP Manager under Territory squad swap. Focus Mode and Overlap are mutually exclusive (turning on one disables the other) since they'd conflict visually on the character cards. No SQL change — pins are stored in the franchise settings blob like other client preferences.
Version 1.6935 — Pinned Spotlight restyled to match the split-group visual treatment. Previously the Spotlight rendered as a plain bordered rectangle with a small "PINNED SPOTLIGHT" badge notched into the top-left corner and two buttons floating in the top-right. Visually it didn't read as part of the same design vocabulary as the rest of the Contracts screen — split groups have a distinctive 5px coloured left stripe, tinted background, and a full-width coloured header bar, and that polish was missing from the Spotlight. Changes: (1) .pinned-section now renders with a 5px --ps-color left stripe (via ::before pseudo-element), tinted background at ~8% alpha (hex +14 suffix, matching split-group's tint math), and a 1.5px coloured border — all three pulling from the same user-chosen colour. (2) New .pinned-section-header full-width bar with min-height:44px, border-bottom:1px solid var(--ps-color), containing: 📌 icon chip on the left, PINNED SPOTLIGHT title in Cinzel uppercase with --ps-color text, ✎ edit pencil (opens colour picker), and Unpin all button. Structure mirrors .split-group-header-row so future changes to the header pattern propagate to both. (3) Removed .pinned-section-badge and .pinned-section-actions classes — they were replaced by the inline header row. The absolute-positioning hacks from v1.6932/v1.6933 are gone; everything lays out via flexbox inside the header. (4) Nested layout: when a split-group is pinned (kind:'splitGroup'), it now sits inside the Spotlight's tinted body with its own coloured stripe and header, creating a clean visual nesting where the Spotlight's colour frames the inner group's colour. New selector rules (.pinned-section > .card, .pinned-section > .split-group, .pinned-section > .pinned-sub-row) give each type of pinned child consistent margins inside the wrapper. No logic change, no SQL change, no data-model change — purely visual.
Version 1.6934 — Split-task modal redesigned around a direct-manipulation cutline instead of per-subtask Stay/Move pills. (1) Interaction model. Subtasks now render in their natural order (no per-row reordering; users can rearrange on the main screen first if needed). A horizontal red dashed line with a scissors handle (✂ Cut here) sits between two adjacent subtasks. Users drag the handle up or down to pick where the task is split; everything above the line stays in the original, everything below moves to the new task. (2) State simplification. Removed P.splitLeft / P.splitRight arrays and their _sid-based tracking. Replaced with a single integer P.splitCutAt (range 1..N−1) that names the index where the split falls. Default position is Math.ceil(N/2) — halfway down, biased so the Stay bucket gets the larger half on odd counts (5 subs → 3 stay, 2 move). (3) Pointer drag. pointerdown on the scissors handle captures the pointer; pointermove finds the closest gap boundary to the finger Y-coordinate (using each subtask row's viewport-relative top from getBoundingClientRect) and snaps the cutline to that gap. Because the render function rebuilds the handle element on every position change, the handler re-captures the pointer on the newly-rendered element so drag continuity is preserved across re-renders. touch-action:none on the handle prevents the browser's native vertical scroll from stealing the gesture on mobile. (4) Accessibility fallback. Small ▲/▼ nudge buttons next to the handle move the cutline one gap at a time via click (one-step per tap, disabled at list extremes). Useful on desktop with a mouse and as a non-drag alternative. (5) Row styling. Items above the line render with the standard parchment background; items below render with a gold-tinted background (rgba(201,146,42,.08)) and a slightly darker border to preview their destination. Section labels above each group show the source name + count (e.g. "Stay in \"Errands\" (3)" / "Move to new task (2)"). (6) Confirm logic. src.subtasks.slice(0, cutAt) stays, slice(cutAt) moves. No _sid resolution needed — natural order is authoritative. Removed CSS: .split-pick-row, .spk-pill/spk-pill-stay/spk-pill-move, .spk-reorder, .spk-section-header, .spk-empty, .spk-name. New CSS: .split-stay-row, .split-move-row, .split-section-lbl, .split-cutline, .split-cut-handle, .split-cut-nudge. No data-model change, no SQL change.
Version 1.6933 — Two display fixes. (1) Literal escape sequences in modal headers. Three modal headers were rendering literal \u2195, \u2715, \u21E1, \u25B2, \u25BC, \u21E3, \u2702, and \uD83D\uDCCC text instead of the intended glyphs (↕ ✕ ⇡ ▲ ▼ ⇣ ✂ 📌). Root cause: Unicode escape sequences only get interpreted as characters inside JavaScript string literals — when placed inside static HTML text content (e.g. <button>\u2195</button>), the browser renders them verbatim since HTML doesn't process JavaScript-style escapes. Slipped up when writing the Reorder modal HTML block (v1.6925), the Split Group picker header (v1.6932), and the Pinned Spotlight picker header (v1.6932). Fix: six button labels + two <h3> headers updated to embed the literal glyphs directly. (2) Pinned Spotlight ✎ edit button hidden behind "Unpin all". v1.6932 absolutely-positioned both action buttons at fixed right-offsets (right:86px for ✎, right:10px for "Unpin all"), which assumed a fixed width for the Unpin all label — but the actual button is wider than 76px so the ✎ sat directly underneath and was unreachable. Fix: wrapped both buttons in a flex container (.pinned-section-actions) anchored to the top-right corner, with gap:4px between them. Buttons now auto-space regardless of label width, and the ✎ is visible and tappable to open the colour picker. No logic change, no SQL change, no data-model change.
Version 1.6932 — Split-group UX upgrade + Pinned Spotlight colour customization. (1) Editable group title. New meta.title field on S.splitGroupMeta[gid] (optional string, falls back to "Split group" placeholder). Picker modal (ov-splitgrp-color) now has a text input at the top that live-saves on every keystroke — typing immediately re-renders the group header with the new title. Persists through the existing split_group_meta jsonb column; no new SQL. (2) Master collapse. Split-group header redesigned from one big button into three separate tap targets: [icon-btn] (opens picker via data-a="sgpick"), [title-btn] (toggles master collapse via new data-a="sgcol"), [pencil-btn] (also opens picker). Collapse state computed as "all members collapsed" → chevron shows ▶; tap flips every member's collapsed flag to the opposite uniform state. (3) Defer + Pin relocated to group level. Tasks inside a split group now render with 5 action buttons instead of 7 — 🕒 Defer and 📌 Pin removed from member action rows. New footer row on the split-group wrapper has group-level 🕒 Defer / 🔥 Return and 📌 Pin / 📍 Pinned buttons that apply to all members simultaneously. New data-a actions: sgcol, sgbrn, sgunbrn, sgpin. taskCardHtml accepts new inSplitGroup param; when true, suppresses defer + pin and shrinks inline grid from 7 to 5 columns. Mobile media query updated with .tacts-in-sg class override so the !important rule doesn't force 7 cols on in-group tasks. (4) Group defer behaviour. Tapping the group defer button opens the existing burner modal with P.brnGroup set to the gid; confirmBurner now has a group-mode branch that writes the same burnerUntil timestamp to every member in lockstep. Un-defer clears all members at once. (5) Group pin = new pin kind. New S.pinned entry type: {kind:'splitGroup', gid}. Spotlight renderer handles this by emitting the full group wrapper (header + members + footer) as a unit. Split-group HTML extracted to renderSplitGroupHtml(gid, members, isManual, nbIdxs) helper so Spotlight and main-body render share the same markup. Orphan-pin cleanup in ensureTaskIds extended: splitGroup pins kept iff the gid has a meta entry AND at least 2 live members. (6) Pinned Spotlight colour customization. New S.settings.pinnedSpotlightColor field drives two CSS custom properties (--ps-color, --ps-tint) on the .pinned-section wrapper — border, background tint, badge text, and hover accents all pull from the single colour. Small ✎ edit button in the header (between "Pinned Spotlight" label and "Unpin all") opens new ov-pinspot-color modal: swatch grid (reuses SGPC_COLORS palette) + Reset button to revert to default gold. Rides in the existing settings jsonb blob; no SQL change. (7) Code health. Split-group render pulled out of the sorted.map inline block — both main-body and Spotlight now call the same helper. Future changes to split-group markup happen in one place instead of two.
Version 1.6931 — Critical hotfix for v1.6930. User reported mobile sync failures with error active_tasks insert 400: code pgrst102 details null hint null message "all object keys must match". Root cause: v1.6930's split_group column was only added to regular active task rows (line ~20622) but not to the two project row types (__proj_meta__ sentinel at ~20635, and per-task project rows at ~20646). PostgREST requires every row in a bulk sbInsert to share the same key set; when S.projects contained anything — which is common because the v1.6924 disable-projects change left existing Supabase data intact — the mixed-shape allRows array made the whole insert fail. No data was lost (the push rejected atomically rather than partially writing), but sync was broken for anyone with legacy project rows in their database. Fix: added split_group:null to both project row types so all rows in the batch share the same shape. Project rows can't be part of split groups (splits only operate on S.active) so null is always correct here. No SQL change needed. No behavior change for users who never had projects. Lesson: future schema additions to active_tasks must update all three row builders in the push loop — a lint check or factory function would prevent a repeat. Noted for backlog.
Version 1.6930 — Critical persistence fix: v1.6924's Split / Merge / Pin features claimed to ride inside existing JSON columns but the push/pull paths were never updated. Result: splitting a task and reloading lost the group association; pinning anything and reloading emptied the Spotlight; split-group colors/emoji reset on every sync. SQL migration required before deploying this version:

ALTER TABLE active_tasks ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS split_group text DEFAULT NULL;
ALTER TABLE franchise ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS split_group_meta jsonb NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb;
ALTER TABLE franchise ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS pinned jsonb NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]'::jsonb;

Code changes: (1) sbPush active_tasks row now includes split_group:t._splitGroup||null. (2) sbPush franchise row now includes split_group_meta:S.splitGroupMeta||{} and pinned:S.pinned||[]. (3) sbPull franchise hydration reads the two new jsonb columns and restores S.splitGroupMeta and S.pinned, with ||{} / ||[] fallbacks for pre-migration rows so no crash on first pull. (4) sbPull active_tasks hydration reads t.split_group into _splitGroup. (5) Code-session login path's _keys blob list now includes splitGroupMeta and pinned so code-account users get persistence via the login_codes.player_data blob. Deployment order: run the SQL in Supabase first, then deploy the HTML. The new columns have sensible defaults so existing franchise rows get {} / [] automatically and the app continues working identically until users create their first split group or pin. What finally works after this: splits survive reloads (both halves remember their group id); pins survive reloads (Pinned Spotlight restores on pull); split group colors/emoji survive reloads. No behavior change for users with no splits or pins. No data migration needed — new columns start empty and get populated by natural app use.
Version 1.6928 — Split groups now get per-group colour + optional emoji, borrowing the visual pattern from the old Projects system. Rationale: even after the v1.6927 subtle redesign, users reported that two tasks inside a split group still read as siblings in a bordered box rather than members of a unit. Stronger visual grouping was needed. (1) Data model. New top-level S.splitGroupMeta dict keyed by _splitGroup id, each entry {color, ic}. Colours round-robin through the Projects palette (PROJ_COLORS) avoiding collisions with existing groups. Icons default to null (renders as a ✂ placeholder); users can pick from a curated emoji list. ensureTaskIds extended to auto-create missing meta entries with non-colliding colours and drop orphan entries for groups that no longer have ≥2 members. Rides inside existing JSON — no SQL change. (2) Render. Split-group wrapper now uses two CSS custom properties (--sg-color, --sg-tint) populated from meta: a 5px coloured left-stripe (was 4px gold), 1.5px coloured outer border, tinted background at ~8% alpha, and a tappable header row with the group's emoji + "SPLIT GROUP" label + a ✎ edit affordance. Member cards inside the group still strip their own chrome so the group reads as one unit, and the dotted divider between members now inherits the group colour too. (3) Picker modal. New ov-splitgrp-color modal repurposes the old Projects picker UI: emoji grid with 28 curated choices plus a "None" reset option, colour swatch grid with 18 swatches. All swatches/emoji are ≥44×44px for touch. Live-saves and re-renders on every tap so the user sees the group update in real time. (4) Two access paths. Per user spec: (a) tap the group header (data-a="sgpick") routes through the existing #tlist click handler, and (b) long-press (500ms hold, 8px move-slop cancel, desktop mousedown fallback) on any member task card opens the same picker. The long-press IIFE ignores touches on buttons, inputs, sub-task rows, the drag handle, pie toggle, and the group header itself so it doesn't compete with existing interactions. (5) Auto-colour on split creation. When the Split modal confirms, ensureTaskIds runs on the next renderTasks call and assigns the new group a colour from the palette that isn't already used by any other live group. Users can re-pick at any time via either access path.
Version 1.6927 — Split group visual redesign. Previous implementation read as "two sibling cards in a bordered box" with a jarring Cinzel uppercase "SPLIT GROUP" label notched into the top border — style clashed with the rest of the Contracts screen and the two member tasks didn't feel connected. New design combines a shared gold left-stripe bracket (reusing the ::before pattern every task card already has) with a warm parchment-tinted background (rgba(201,146,42,.06)) filling the whole group. Inner cards inside a split group have their own borders, left-stripe, box-shadow, and background stripped so they visually merge into the group as members of one unit rather than siblings. A thin dotted divider (1px dotted rgba(139,96,16,.25)) separates members — same pattern as sub-task row dividers inside a task, reinforcing the "parts of a whole" read. The badge is now just a tiny ✂ icon (no text, .68em, gold-dark, opacity:.75) tucked into the top-left corner pocket between the gold stripe and the first card's content. New CSS selectors: .split-group::before, .split-group > .card + .card for the divider. No data model changes, no SQL change. No changes to Split, Merge, Pin, or Reorder logic — purely visual.
Version 1.6926 — Two mobile fixes for the v1.6924/6925 Contracts overhaul. (1) Multi-line sub-task alignment. On long sub-task names that wrap to multiple lines, the drag handle (⠿), pie-progress toggle (◐), checkbox, and ⋯ menu dot were vertically centered relative to the whole wrapped block, leaving them floating in the middle of the row. Changed .strow-main from align-items:center to align-items:flex-start and added per-element padding-top (~8-10px) on the handle / pie / dot so they visually sit on the first line of text. Label contents also top-align now. (2) Split / Merge / Reorder modal overflow on mobile. Modals had padding:0;overflow:hidden inline, which overrode the base .modal's max-height:86vh; overflow-y:auto — on a phone with enough sub-tasks, the footer (Cancel + Split/Merge buttons) rendered below the viewport and couldn't be reached. Fix: all three modals restructured as flex columns with max-height:86vh on the outer wrapper, flex-shrink:0 on header/footer/fixed sections, and flex:1; overflow-y:auto on only the scrollable inner list. Footer is now always pinned at the bottom of the modal regardless of list length; inner list scrolls independently. Same structural fix applied to the Reorder modal for consistency even though it didn't have a footer. No data-model changes, no SQL change.
Version 1.6925 — Mobile-first polish for the v1.6924 Contracts overhaul. (1) Sub-task row swipe drawers. Trailing controls (⇄ move, 📌 pin, ✕ delete) removed from the row body; they now live in two absolutely-positioned drawers that slide out via swipe gesture. Swipe left reveals the right drawer (⇄ + 📌), swipe right reveals the left drawer (✕), partial swipes below a 50px threshold snap closed. A subtle ⋯ button on the right edge of each row taps-open the action drawer for swipe-averse users. Outside-tap closes any open drawer. Swipe handler is attached via delegation on #tlist (touchstart/touchmove/touchend) and explicitly excludes touches starting on .st-drag-handle so the existing long-press drag-to-reorder still works. Horizontal-vs-vertical direction check (1.5× ratio) means vertical scrolling isn't hijacked. New CSS classes: .strow-main, .strow-actions, .strow-del, .strow-dot, .strow-drawer-btn with .sd-move / .sd-pin / .sd-del variants. The .strow wrapper now has overflow:hidden; position:relative; touch-action:pan-y;. (2) +gp conditional on unchecked. The +N gp label on each sub-task now only renders when the sub-task is incomplete (built into the row HTML so no extra CSS needed). Compromise answer to mobile space-pressure — completed sub-tasks get the full row width for their strikethrough name. (3) Task action row collapsed from 9 → 7 buttons. The ▼ ▲ ⇡ trio (move down / up / to top) replaced with a single ↕ Reorder button (data-a="treorder") that opens the new ov-task-reorder modal. The modal shows the full non-burner task list with the source task highlighted, plus ⇡ To Top / ▲ Up / ▼ Down / ⇣ To Bottom action buttons (44px min). reorderApply(action) operates on S.active indices directly and live-refreshes the modal after each move so the user can see the task's new position before closing. Disabled states handle top/bottom edge cases. Burner tasks still float to the bottom automatically via sortedActive. (4) Task counter hidden on mobile. The .tacts .tsub cell (0/N sub-tasks / N gp/tick · N ticks) now hides below 640px via @media rule. Same info is visible elsewhere (sub-task checkmarks, gold button tooltip). (5) Desktop grid simplified. Both duplicated .tacts rules at lines 148 and 1916 went from repeat(9,minmax(0,1fr)) back to repeat(7,...); inline grid in taskCardHtml matches; .tsub grid-column resets from 10 to 8. No data-model changes. No SQL change.
Version 1.6924 — Contracts screen overhaul: Projects feature replaced with Split / Merge / Pin. (1) Split (✂): tasks with 2+ sub-tasks can be split into two tasks via a picker modal (ov-task-split) with a two-column sub-task picker, new-task name input, and up/down reorder within each column. Both halves share a _splitGroup id and render inside a bordered .split-group wrapper with a tiny ✂ badge notching the top border. The new task is inserted at srcIdx+1 so the pair stays adjacent. (2) Merge (⚭): two active tasks with sub-tasks can be merged into one master via ov-task-merge — target dropdown (excludes the source and anything with zero sub-tasks), new-title input that auto-defaults to "<source> + <target>" until the user edits it (dirty flag), and a combined sub-task list with drag-to-reorder plus A/B origin pills. On confirm: source keeps its _tid and position, counts sum, max pct taken, notes concatenated with ·, target removed from S.active, _splitGroup cleared on the merged result. (3) Pin (📌/📍): every task action row and every sub-task row gets a thumbtack button. Pinned items are stored in new top-level array S.pinned[] as {kind:'task'|'sub', tid, sid?} references — never copies. The Pinned Spotlight renders at the top of the Contracts screen (.pinned-section with gold border), re-rendering the source element so checking off a pinned sub-task updates the original in lockstep. Includes an "Unpin all" link. New data fields: subtask._sid (stable sub-task id, backfilled in ensureTaskIds), task._splitGroup, top-level S.pinned[]. All ride inside existing active_tasks JSON — no SQL change. Pin collapse on merge (Q3 decision): task-level pins on either source collapse into one pin of the merged task (deduped by kind+tid+sid); sub-task pins already reference _sid which rides along with the subtask through merge. Cleanup: ensureTaskIds now strips _splitGroup from tasks that end up as the sole remaining member of their group (e.g. after delete or merge-away), and drops orphan S.pinned entries whose tid/sid no longer resolves. UI cleanup: Sort dropdown removed (hidden stub preserved for JS compat; sortedActive reads defensively and hardcodes manual), 📁 Projects button hidden, 🗂➕ Move-to-Project button removed from action row, instructional hints condensed to a single line plus ℹ help modal (ov-contracts-help). Archive button kept, now on its own right-aligned row. Action row grid: 7 → 9 columns (▼ ▲ ⇡ 🕒 ✂ ⚭ 📌 🗂 💰); .tsub counter cell moved from grid-column:8 → grid-column:10; both duplicated CSS blocks (lines 148 and 1916) updated; mobile .tacts grid 6 → 5 columns to accommodate 2-row wrap of the 9-button set. Projects preservation: #s-projects screen, all project modals, S.projects data model, and renderProjects / project helpers all left intact — just unreferenced. Any existing project rows in Supabase remain untouched. The to-proj click handler branch is kept in a block comment so restoration is one edit away. Mobile revision (same version): Split modal redesigned from side-by-side two-column to single-column stacked list — each row has a tap-to-toggle Stay/Move pill (min 44×36px) plus ↑/↓ reorder buttons that operate within the row's current bucket. Section headers ("Stay in X (N)" / "Move to new task (N)") separate the two groups. Merge modal row height bumped to 44px min with ≥36px tall reorder buttons; drag handle hidden below 480px viewport since HTML5 drag doesn't fire on touch browsers (desktop drag still works).
Version 1.6923 — Fix: auto-grind QP-spend and grind assignment modal were pulling characters from squads committed to non-active cycles, violating the rule that a squad can only be assigned to one cycle at a time. Root cause: both the Grinds modal's display pool (line ~7770) and the auto-assign handler (line ~15797) built their eligibility pool as (active-cycle-squad ∪ grind-clocked-squads ∪ every character not on a live pending engagement). That last bucket silently included members of squads committed to non-active cycles — those chars aren't on a live pending right now, but their squad is still on loan to a cycle. Fix: new canonical helper computeGrindEligibility() builds the pool as (active-cycle-squad-members ∪ grind-clocked-squad-members ∪ characters not in ANY squad at all) minus busy chars. Replaced three duplicated pool sites (Grinds modal display, auto-assign handler, and the manual grind-assignment modal's character picker) to all call the new helper. Backstops: renderQuestAssignmentList's grind branch now filters the picker list through the eligibility pool (ineligible chars are hidden, not just greyed); both best-fit scoring sites restrict their recommendation pool the same way; and confirmQuestAssignment added a final hard-reject backstop so no slipped-through selection can commit a rule-violating grind. A character in a squad committed to Cycle B can now only grind if either (a) Cycle B is activated, (b) Cycle B's squad is clocked into grinds, or (c) the character is removed from that squad entirely. No SQL change. No behavior change for characters already legitimately eligible.
Version 1.6922 — Four changes across the Quest Map, character modal, party panel, and contracts screen. (1) Quest Log replaces Activate on completed cycles + cycle reorder. On a fully-completed cycle card in the Quest Map, the "⚔ Activate Cycle" button is replaced with a "📜 Quest Log" button that opens a new ov-cycle-log modal — a read-only per-cycle listing of every quest with ✓/○ status, title, description, base GP, and min-level. Completed cycles now render BELOW the Grind Missions card, with a "✓ Completed Cycles" sub-header labeling the section. The render split renderCycleMapCards into two buckets (incompleteCards / completedCards) emitted as: incomplete → Grind → completed. (2) Back-to-HQ abandon warning. Clicking 🏠 Back to HQ on a character who is currently on any active quest or grind now opens a new ov-abandon-warn modal listing each engagement with a verdict per engagement: "✗ Quest will FAIL" (crimson) if removing this character breaks prereqs/hard-gates, or "⚠ Share forfeited" (gold) if the remaining roster can still complete. Verdicts computed via classifyAbandonImpact, which re-runs questPrereqCheck + questHardGateIssues (or grindRosterStatus for grinds) against roster-minus-this-char. On confirm, applyAbandonImpact either splices the char (support) or removes the pending entry and clears qs.assigned (prereq fail); every action logs to the adventure log. New helper getPendingEngagementsForChar(ci) scans the active cycle's live qs.pending/grindPending plus all non-active cycles' setProgress[*]. Characters not on any engagement fall through to the original simple confirm path. Downtime is already handled by its own forfeit modal (v1.6915). (3) Squad gold-out indicator. Each squad panel's summary row (next to "N members · N items") now shows a 💰 ~N gp out pill when any member is on a pending engagement. Sum is the franchise reward (pq.reward) across every pending across every cycle that has at least one current squad member in its chars[]; each pending counted once even if multiple squad members are on it. (4) Subtask read-more. Subtask names longer than 120 characters truncate on a word boundary with a "… read more" link; clicking toggles full expansion with "show less". Expanded state kept in-memory (window._subExp) keyed by "taskIdx_subIdx" — cleared on reload since it's a viewing preference, not persisted. Advanced devs can tune the threshold by setting window._subtaskTruncLen. No SQL change across any of these.
Version 1.6921 — Two UI additions, both from backlog. (1) Character modal shows contact quest quota. Overview tab now includes a "Contract: N of Q quests remaining" line when the character is a contact NPC with questsRemaining tracked. Crimson and labeled "final quest" when only 1 left; teal otherwise. Skipped entirely for non-contact characters or contacts without a quota. Read-only surfacing — mirrors the data the v1.6915 Begin warning uses, so you can plan Javvox-style NPC deployments without waiting for the modal. (2) Cycle-owned real estate grouped on Party screen. Buildings purchased through the Quest Map's 🏘 Real Estate flow (v1.6910) are flagged with meta.ownerType:'cycle' and meta.ownerRef:setId. Previously they appeared mixed into the general Purchased Assets list, making it unclear which franchise cycle owned which building. Now the Purchased Assets list excludes them (same treatment squad-owned assets already get), and a new collapsible-styled card appears per cycle between Purchased Assets and Franchise Storage — one per owning cycle, titled e.g. "🏘 Root & Branch · Real Estate" with a building-count badge. Each row shows the building's custom name + the base type (e.g. "Safe House"). Read-only; all mutations continue to route through the Quest Map's Real Estate modal. The "total gp invested" summary still sums every owned asset (cycle-owned included) since they're a real investment. No SQL change.
Version 1.6920 — Fix: limited-contact warning modal (v1.6915) was opening beneath the quest-assignment modal (ov-quest). Both overlays use the base .ov stylesheet rule at z-index:500 — so the warning's dim background tinted darker but the modal itself stayed visually hidden behind the already-open quest modal. Fix: ov-contact-warn raised to z-index:510, same pattern as ov-cpk-inv (v1.6900) and other stacked modals. Also raised the downtime-forfeit modal ov-dt-forfeit — it has the same latent stacking bug since it also opens over ov-quest. No SQL change. No other behavioral changes.
Version 1.6919 — Diagnostic pass for the v1.6918 contact-warning bug. User reported the warning still didn't fire after the v1.6918 SQL + client changes. Two additions to narrow the cause: (1) Console logging in confirmQuestAssignment's pre-flight check — every Begin click now logs each assigned character's contact flag, hireling flag, questsRemaining type and value, and questQuota. A second log line reports how many limited contacts were detected. This tells us whether the field is missing entirely (Supabase persistence still broken), wrong type (stored as string instead of number), or zero (already exhausted). (2) window.setContactQuests console utility — lets you manually reset Javvox or any Contact's remaining/quota without touching the database or NPC editor. Usage: setContactQuests('Javvox', 6) sets both to 6; setContactQuests('Javvox', 3, 6) sets remaining=3 of 6; setContactQuests('Javvox') reads without changing. Runs save() after mutation. Useful for confirming the warning modal itself works in isolation from the round-trip persistence. No SQL change beyond what v1.6918 already added.
Version 1.6918 — Fix: the v1.6915 limited-contact Begin warning wasn't firing even when an assigned Contact had a quest-limit counter set. Root cause: questsRemaining is only ever written to S.party[i].questsRemaining in memory — the party-table Supabase INSERT at line 18750 serialized about 15 fields (name, class, stats, xp, is_contact, ...) but not this one. So Javvox-style Contacts held their value for a single session, then on any subsequent sbPull the field was dropped. The warning check typeof c.questsRemaining !== 'number' saw undefined and correctly returned null (no warning). Same pattern as the v1.543 Bag of Holding bug. Fix (client, 3 sites): (1) push path now serializes quests_remaining and quest_quota columns in the party INSERT payload; (2) pull mapper restores both fields with legacy-meta fallback plus a heal path — if quest_quota is missing but quests_remaining is present (saves written before this version), the quota is backfilled to the current remaining count so the display isn't empty; (3) capstone NPC creation now stamps both questsRemaining and questQuota at awardCapstonReward time. Warning text improved: when quota is known, the modal shows "N of Q quests remaining (including this one)" rather than just the remaining count; the crimson final-quest line now reads "will depart after this quest (last of Q)". Falls back to the older format when quota is unknown. SQL required this time: run add-contact-quest-quota-columns.sql to add quests_remaining and quest_quota integer columns to the party table — idempotent, safe to re-run, includes an optional backfill from a legacy meta blob if one exists.
Version 1.6917 — Completes the Contact-eligibility rollout. After v1.6915/6916 fixed the peg-slot level match and the questHardGateIssues topLevel computation, the Begin button was still disabled on a quest staffed by Javvox (a Lv 4 Contact) because regularQuestRosterStatus — the "at least one Level 1+ adventurer" gate — still filtered out Contacts via the raw !c.hireling check. The info card showed the contradictory state of "✓ Level 4+ adventurer" (from the already-fixed difficulty gate) alongside "Difficulty gate not met" (from this roster-status gate). The v1.6915 design note said roster-composition gates should stay strict, but that was wrong for Contacts: the narrative intent of those gates is "don't let a quest be staffed entirely by 50gp disposable hirelings," which Contacts are not. Four more sites now use _charCountsForLevelSlot: (a) regularQuestRosterStatus — Contacts qualify as the required Lv 1+ adventurer; (b) grindRosterStatus — Contacts qualify as the solo-adventurer path, but are NOT counted toward the "two hirelings pair" path (which is explicitly for 50gp disposable bodies); (c) grindReadiness available pool; (d) questDifficultyRequirementMet and (e) averageLevelForIndices for tier/avg-level calculations. Helper's inline policy note rewritten to reflect the unified rule: all "is this a proper adventurer" gates now treat Contacts as qualifying; only level-up-UI remains strict (_showLvUp correctly hides the Level Up button on Contacts since they're not upgradeable via player gold). No SQL change.
Version 1.6916 — Follow-up to v1.6915: Contacts now also count toward the difficulty gate and related party-level calculations. After v1.6915, a Lv 4 Contact like Javvox could be placed on a Lv 4+ peg slot (the slot border turned green), but questHardGateIssues still computed its topLevel with the raw !c.hireling filter and reported "Missing: Party level 4+ (highest: 0)" — blocking the Begin button. Four more sites updated to use _charCountsForLevelSlot: (a) questHardGateIssues topLevel loop, (b) its busyHighLevel loop (controls the "your highest eligible adventurer is on another quest" message), (c) partyMaxLevel (used for tier selection and reward-range display), (d) questCalcReward topLevel loop (level bonus in gold reward). The "⬆ Level Up" peg button's !c.hireling gate is kept as-is — Contacts aren't level-upable through the player-pays-gold UI, and since they carry hireling:true the existing check correctly hides the button. buildTestRoster also kept raw: test rosters never include Contacts. No SQL change.
Version 1.6915 — Two Contact-related changes. (1) Fix: capstone-reward NPC Contacts count toward level prereqs. Root cause: capstone NPCs are created with hireling:true, contact:true so they inherit hireling-style defaults (zero hire cost, no level-up upsell). But six level-prereq sites gated eligibility with a raw !c.hireling check, so a Lv 4 Contact like "Javvox" was rejected from a Lv 4+ peg slot despite clearly meeting the requirement. New _charCountsForLevelSlot(c) helper: true for non-hirelings AND for hireling:true + contact:true NPCs; false for standard hirelings (which still cap at Lv 0). Applied at six sites: qddCharCoversSlot peg match (the direct symptom), prereq-row level met check (assigned pool + full pool), info-card level met check (assigned + full pool), characterMeetsAssignmentLevel, characterCanMeetAssignmentLevelAfterOneLevel, and the "meets level" pill in the adventurer pick list. Deliberately unchanged: grindRosterStatus and regularQuestRosterStatus still use the raw !c.hireling check because those gates enforce "the squad has at least one real adventurer" — that's a roster-composition policy separate from the level question. (2) Feature: limited-contact Begin warning. Contacts with a tracked questsRemaining counter are consumed by each quest and depart when it hits zero (existing mechanic, v1.666). Previously a user could click Begin and not realize they were burning a contact's last slot. Now on Begin: if any peg-assigned character is a contact with questsRemaining > 0, a new ov-contact-warn modal surfaces with one row per limited contact showing "N quests remaining (including this one)" (or "will depart after this quest" in crimson if only 1 left), a per-contact ✕ Unassign button that clears them from all peg slots without committing, plus Cancel and Begin Anyway footer actions. confirmQuestAssignment gained a skipContactWarn parameter so Begin Anyway doesn't re-prompt on re-entry; the downtime-forfeit gate still runs normally. No SQL change: Contact data already carries contact:true + questsRemaining from the NPC contact editor.
Version 1.6914 — Fix: phantom empty Faith peg slot on the quest-assignment modal (e.g. "Cellar Savant" showed a ✝ FAITH slot with no deity named). Root cause: shape drift between the Quest Editor's quest-matrix chip-picker (writes pre.rel as a JSON array) and the rest of the codebase (expects a single string or ""). The main app's peg-slot guard at qddBuildCharSlots only defended against null/undefined/""; empty arrays slipped through because [] is truthy AND [] !== "", so a Faith slot was pushed with slot.value = []. The render coerced esc([]) to the empty string and produced a bordered slot with a label but no visible requirement. Same drift also broke the single-faith quests (Ravenous, Hope Springs Eternal, Cold Trail, Rune Corrupted) whose pre.rel was ["Faith Name"] instead of "Faith Name" \u2014 peg-fill checks like c.rel===slot.value compared a string to an array and never matched, making the slots un-fillable. Fix (3 layers): (1) qddBuildCharSlots at line 23355 now accepts both array and string shapes, skips blank/whitespace/non-string entries, and trims. (2) mergeCustomContent now normalizes pre.rel to a single string at merge time \u2014 so all 10+ downstream consumers (grindReadiness, confirm-path best-fit scoring, prereq row rendering, hireling suggestions, etc.) see a canonical shape without per-site patching. (3) Quest Editor (v1.679) now writes pre.rel as a string consistently \u2014 both the form save path and the questToRow serializer coerce to the first non-empty string on save, preventing new bad data. One-time SQL cleanup provided as normalize-rel-shape.sql: flattens existing array-shaped pre.rel values across all quest rows. Dry-run preview + commented UPDATE; idempotent, safe to re-run. After the client fixes alone, rendering works correctly regardless of DB shape; the SQL is optional tidy-up to canonicalize the stored data.
Version 1.6913 — Grind refactor: grinds are now a unified, cycle-independent list reached through the Quest Map. (1) Adventure screen stripped of grinds. The per-cycle adventure view no longer renders the ⚙ Grind Missions section, its auto-assign button, in-progress cards, or [data-grind-header] anchor. Per-cycle screens show only that cycle's quests. The dead pre-block auto-assign button tied to use-before-def _canAutoGrind/_grindAutoCost (never actually rendered due to var hoisting) was also removed. (2) New dedicated Grinds modal ov-grinds-list: summary band (clocked-in squads · eligible characters · in-progress count), auto-assign button, all grinds sorted in-progress → ready → stretch → overreach → reward desc. Owns its own 1s countdown tick (grindsCountdownTimer) while open; re-renders when a mission crosses zero. (3) Ten new harder grinds (g12\u2013g21) with sliding difficulty: rewards 45\u2013100 gp, enforced minLevel 2\u20136, enforced minSquadSize 2\u20134, progressive prereqs (single-skill \u2192 multi-skill + tools + languages). grindRosterStatus() signature extended with a second grindObj parameter (backward-compatible when omitted) and now rejects rosters under a grind's minSquadSize; all four callers updated (grindReadiness, auto-assign loop, and both confirm-path checks). The Grinds modal surfaces minLevel and minSquadSize in each card's prereq line alongside skills/tools/languages. (4) Click handlers rebound to document scope. gassign / gcollect / grind-auto branches moved out of #avcontent's click listener into a fresh document-scoped listener so they fire from the new modal. Each mutation also triggers renderGrindsList() when the modal is visible so the view stays current without a manual reopen; confirmQuestAssignment's grind path does the same. Auto-assign's character pool was widened to match the Grinds modal's eligibility rules (active cycle + clocked-in + unassigned), replacing the old active-cycle-only pool. (5) Entry points re-wired. Quest Map \u201cOpen Grinds\u201d button no longer navigates to the Adventure screen and scrolls to a now-missing anchor \u2014 it calls openGrindsList() and closes the map modal. The v1.6912 party-row free-chooser\u2019s Grind path does the same. No SQL change: new grind entries are static data on GRIND[], and pending grind entries continue to store only id \u2014 minLevel/minSquadSize are looked up from GRIND[] at check time.
Version 1.6912 — Two coordinated features. (1) Quest-Editor Real Estate deny rules consumer. The Quest Editor (v1.678) now lets writers deny specific buildings (or the whole catalog) per completed cycle via a realEstateDeny field on the cycle's data JSONB blob — no SQL change needed. Main app now honors it: realEstateCatalog(setId) takes a setId and filters the curated list against the cycle's deny rules. New helpers realEstateDenyForSet(setId) and realEstateAllDeniedForSet(setId). renderRealEstateCatalog shows a clear 🚫 "Real estate unavailable" notice when the whole cycle is denied. The 🏘 Real Estate button on Quest Map cycle cards is hidden when all real estate is denied AND no buildings have already been assigned to the cycle; if owned buildings exist, the button stays available so users can still view them. (2) Party-row free chooser. Clicking the dotted-underline "free" status on a squad roster row no longer jumps straight to the downtime modal — it now opens ov-free-choice, a compact picker with 🕯 Downtime and ⚙ Grind Mission options. Downtime routes to the existing startDowntimeForChar(ci) unchanged. Grind option is hard-gated by _charCanGrind(ci): eligible only when the character is in a squad currently clocked in to the grind shift OR not in any squad (unassigned/ad-hoc); when ineligible the Grind button is hidden and the modal message explains why (either grinds not yet unlocked, or squad not clocked in). Confirming Grind navigates to the Adventure screen and scrolls to the grind section via the existing [data-grind-header] anchor. Same pattern as Quest Map's "Open Grinds" button. Gate is re-checked at confirm time as defense-in-depth.
Version 1.6911 — Fix: abandoning an active quest cycle caused previously-completed cycles (e.g. Root & Branch 10/10) to visually regress to a partial count matching the abandoned cycle's progress (e.g. 3/10). Root cause: the abandon handler cleared qs.setId, qs.pending, and qs.grindPending but left qs.done, qs.repeatCounts, qs.revealed, and qs.assigned holding the abandoned cycle's indices. With setId=null, activeQuestSet() falls back to QUESTSETS[0] (Root & Branch) — so render code then evaluated the stale indices against a different set's quests. Because quest indices (0, 1, 2, …) collide across sets, the first N quests of the fallback set appeared falsely complete. The true progress for both cycles was still correct in setProgress[setId] — only the live qs.* arrays were corrupt, so the regression was cosmetic but persistent (survived save/reload). Previously latent because players rarely lingered in the no-active-cycle state between abandon and re-activate; v1.6910's Real Estate button and Grind Missions card on the Quest Map invited exactly that browsing pattern, exposing the bug. Fix (abandon handler): when isActive and we null qs.setId, also reset qs.done=[], qs.repeatCounts={}, qs.revealed=[], qs.assigned={}. The cycle's persisted progress in setProgress[setId] (written one line above by saveActiveSetProgress()) is untouched, so re-activating the same cycle via Quest Map restores the correct state through the normal loadSetProgress() path. Fix (self-heal): added healOrphanedQuestState() — runs at load time alongside sanitizeDowntimeKeys() and migrateArtificerToRunesmith() across all three load paths (local load(), code-login, and sbPull). If qs.setId is null/missing but any of done / repeatCounts / revealed / assigned hold entries, those live arrays are cleared. Idempotent; no-op when state is legitimate. Self-heals any save already corrupted by the old handler so existing users recover on first load without manual re-activation.
Version 1.6910 — Quest Map overhaul redirecting access to Grind Missions and repurposing completed ("old") cycles as real-estate destinations. Data model: cycleSquadMap() now recognizes a special '__grind__' key whose value is an array of squad names (multi-squad clock-in). All existing setId → string entries are unchanged; the stale-cleanup pass now dedupes the grind array and handles arrays vs strings per key. New helpers: grindSquadNames(), isSquadClockedInToGrinds(), clockInSquadToGrinds(), clockOutSquadFromGrinds(), availableSquadsForGrindClockIn(), grindsUnlocked() (gated on isSetCompleted('root-and-branch')), plus a split of squadIsLockedByCycle() into a narrow squadIsLockedByActiveCycle() (excludes grinds) and the original back-compat wrapper (still used by squad-manager roster gates, which correctly reject both cycle- and grind-locked squads). squadsAssignedToOtherCycles() now also excludes squads clocked into grinds, so cycle activation can't steal them. Quest Map card changes: (1) Grind Missions card is now interactive — renders clocked-in squad chips with ✕ clock-out buttons, an "➕ Add Squad" button (opens new ov-grind-clockin picker), and an "⚡ Open Grinds" button that navigates to the Adventure screen and scroll-targets the new data-grind-header anchor. Locked state shown when Root & Branch isn't complete. (2) Completed cycle cards get a 🏘 Real Estate button (opens new ov-cycle-realestate catalog modal) and a building-count badge in the header. (3) Completed-cycle expanded bodies now list assigned buildings inline. Real Estate catalog: new realEstateCatalog() returns a curated 10-item subset of BUILDINGS across 200–3000 gp price points (Storage Shed, Stables, Market Stall, Barracks, Smithy, Tavern, Greenhouse, Alchemist's Lab, Library, Safe House). Purchase flow uses goldSpend(), pushes an asset with meta.ownerType:'cycle' and meta.ownerRef:setId, then opens a pet-purchase-style ov-cre-name naming modal (Skip / Save / Enter-to-save). cycleAssignedBuildings(setId) enumerates them for display. Adventure screen gate widened: the grind section previously required doneCount ≥ totalQ on the active cycle; it now also renders when grindsUnlocked() && grindSquadNames().length > 0. The auto-assign eligibility pool unions active-cycle squad + every clocked-in grind squad + unassigned characters. Grind assignment modal: populateQuestSquadPicker() detects P4.grind and swaps in a grind-specific squad list (active cycle squad prefixed with ⚔, clocked-in grind squads prefixed with ⚙, plus a 🧭 Unassigned (ad-hoc) sentinel that bypasses the squad auto-check-and-lock). applyQuestSquadSelection() treats '__unassigned__' specially so all peg slots stay unlocked for ad-hoc picking. The gassign handler no longer force-disables the dropdown, and confirmQuestAssignment() now reads the dropdown value for the grind path (storing '' for ad-hoc) instead of blindly persisting activeCycleSquadName().
Version 1.6906 — Renamed the Artificer class to Runesmith across both character generators. Six sites updated in the main app (the DND.cls list, plus the class-keyed maps for skills, base stats, starting equipment, weapon proficiencies, and inventory) and two in the Quest Editor (class-stats map, class list). Because c.cls is used as a data key throughout — e.g. CLASS_SKILLS[c.cls], CLASS_STATS[c.cls] — a pure rename would have stranded any saved character with cls:'Artificer' (all their class-driven lookups would silently miss). Added migrateArtificerToRunesmith() that rewrites cls to 'Runesmith' on S.party, any pending capstone-NPC queue, and partyAssets.meta (both direct .cls and nested .npc.cls shapes). The migration is idempotent and runs from all three load paths alongside sanitizeDowntimeKeys() — local load(), code-login, and the main sbPull — so corrupted save data heals on first session open.
Version 1.6905 — Party-screen roster status was wrong for any character on a non-active quest cycle. Root cause: three independent scans (questBusyForChar, the questingChars/downtimeChars map at the top of renderParty, and the _msUntilFree helper inside each squad panel) only read S.questState.pending and S.questState.grindPending — which hold the active cycle's live state only. Per the v1.551/v1.476 infrastructure, non-active cycles' pending quests and grind missions live in S.questState.setProgress[setId].pending / .grindPending. So squads assigned to any cycle other than the current active one rendered "free" regardless of whether their characters were actually out on quests. All four call sites now iterate every setProgress[*] entry in addition to the live active-cycle arrays, using the minimum remaining doneAt/endsAt across every source for the time-until-free label. The "click a busy status" ov-char-status modal (v1.6902) was fixed in the same pass: it now finds matches across all cycles, captures the match's setId, and resolves the correct QUESTSETS entry by that id instead of always calling activeQuestSet() — so a non-active-cycle character's modal now shows the correct quest title and set name. Cycle number is omitted when the match isn't the currently-active cycle (non-active setProgress entries don't carry a comparable counter).
Version 1.6904 — Downtime rolls no longer pop an inline dice modal at every completion. (1) runDowntimeCompletion now queues completed downtimes into S.pendingDowntimeRolls and the actual d20 + state mutation is deferred to resolveDowntimeRoll(pending, roll). (2) A new ribbon — "🎲 Downtime completed ×N — tap to roll" — sits between the header and the dev bar and is visible only when the queue is non-empty. Clicking it opens ov-dt-pending, which lists each queued downtime (character · track) and offers a single "🎲 Roll All" button. (3) New runMultiD20Animation draws N independent wireframe d20s in a wider 540×240 arena (ov-dtp-svg); independent positions, velocities, spin drag, and rest-position easing so they settle spread across the floor. Returns an array of N integers; each is piped into resolveDowntimeRoll for its matching queue entry. (4) Every resolved roll appends to S.downtimeRollHistory (capped at 200 entries) with character, track, roll, mods, total, gain, unlock flag, unlock message, and a human-readable timestamp. (5) New Franchise Dashboard tile — 🕯 Downtime Ledger — sits next to Quartermaster with matching chrome (teal gradient instead of navy); shows rolls / unlocks / pending count. Its detail modal mirrors the Quartermaster layout exactly: three-stat bar, dashed-row history list, Cinzel caps dividers. (6) Ribbon re-renders on app load (both cloud and offline paths) so queued rolls from a prior session surface immediately. renderDowntimeRibbon, openDowntimePendingModal, and wiring run once on DOMContentLoaded with _wired guards to avoid double-binding.
Version 1.6903 — Two fixes. (1) Party search now has an ✕ clear button at the right edge of the input that shows only when the field has content. Clicking it empties the input, re-renders the party (restoring the squad panels), and blurs the field. Input now has right-side padding to keep text from colliding with the button. (2) Continue Downtime bulk-resume panel was displaying only the first word of each character's name via name.split(' ')[0] — characters like "The Founder" were rendering as just "The," obscuring whose downtime was being resumed. Both the per-character row labels and the confirmation message now use the full name.
Version 1.6902 — Party screen interactivity pass. (1) Squad roster rows: the status readout on the right side of each row (free / ⚔ X hr / 🕯 X hr) is now a dotted-underline clickable button. For free characters it opens the downtime assignment modal via startDowntimeForChar(ci). For questing characters it opens a new ov-char-status modal showing the active cycle number, quest-set name, quest title, squad, other party members on the mission, and time to return. Grind missions render as "Grind Mission · title" since they aren't in a cycle/set. For downtime characters, the same modal shows the downtime track label (via downtimeTrackLabel), session length, track progress, and time to completion. (2) Removed the "Available" filter button from the party toolbar (kept __not_questing__ tag logic for backward compat in case future filters reuse it). (3) Party search revived: it had been vestigial since char cards were removed in v1.6898. It now scans a rich per-character haystack (name, species, class, background, faith, skills, tools, languages, weapons, inventory, squad names, downtime badges, earned XP badge names, and role flags like hireling/merc/contact/risen/first-hire) and renders the top 5 ranked matches at the top of the Party screen. While the search box is non-empty, the squad panels below are suppressed so only results show. Scoring is multi-token AND (all tokens must match), with a name-match bonus. Each result is a clickable row that opens the character modal.
Version 1.6901 — Five polish fixes. (1) Contracts screen: task collapse arrows (▶/▼) now force text-style rendering via Unicode variation selector \uFE0E. Previously iOS/Safari rendered them as the blue play-emoji; they now match desktop's flat black arrow. (2) Sub-task drag-to-reorder hold time reduced from 500ms to 300ms (standard touch long-press). (3) Add-sub-task field moved to the top of the sub-task list, and newly added sub-tasks now unshift (insert at the top) instead of push (append). Both the "+" button and Enter-key paths updated. The qpScheduleVest tid now references index 0 instead of the old tail index. (4) Party screen: removed the "+ Squad" toolbar button (squad creation still available via ⚔ Manage Squads) and removed the per-squad "Filter by" button from squad panel summaries (filtering is available via the other tag-based filter buttons). (5) Capstone NPC contact rewards: the contact's inventory is now fully marked as personal (ownedItems) on grant, so their gear shows with the 🔒 lock icon and can't be sold or moved to franchise storage. Added a one-time migration at load time that backfills ownedItems for any existing NPC contact whose inventory isn't already personal-flagged.
Version 1.6900 — Five refinements to the character modal. (1) Overview tab tidied: removed redundant Identity block and Squad Membership section (both already shown in the modal header). Replaced with a slim Background + Coin Purse line (gold-tinted purse value). Added a chip row of role/status badges: ⚔ Questing, ⭐ Merc, 🪓 Hireling, 🎖 Risen, 🤝 Contact, 🧭 First Hire. Only chips matching true flags render. Modal header font bumped — title 1.05em → 1.35em, subtitle .72em → .82em. (2) Fixed XP/downtime badge click failure. Root cause: primary delegated click handler was bound to #plist, but the char modal lives outside #plist, so those events never reached it. Added parallel dispatch inside the #ov-charpeek-body scoped listener so XP and downtime badge buttons in the Overview tab open their detail modals (ov-xpbadge, z:510) correctly. (3) Gear tab: renamed "Weapons" header to "Weapon Proficiencies" to reflect that the values are proficiencies, not currently-equipped weapons. (4) Inventory "tap to manage" now opens a proper stacked modal (ov-cpk-inv at z:510) with full-width Sell and Move to Storage buttons, each showing the relevant context inline (e.g. sell value in gp). Replaces the previous inline drawer that appeared below the shared items list. Storage button disabled with tooltip when no building assets are owned. (5) RIF renamed to "Back to HQ" with new semantics: cost 10 QP (was level-scaled GP), the character stays in the party but leaves every squad (was spliced from roster entirely). Auto-disables to "Already at HQ" when the character isn't in any squad. No longer gated by Founder / on-quest / in-downtime — those all allow leaving squad. Graceful fallback alerts when QP is disabled or the balance is insufficient. After success, the char modal's header and Overview refresh so the squad-list line updates immediately.
Version 1.6899 — Restores Level Up and RIF actions that lost their home when char cards were removed in v1.6898. The character modal footer now includes: ⬆ Level Up (left-aligned), ✂ RIF (right-aligned before Close), Close. Both buttons display the cost inline (e.g. "Level Up (125 gp)", "RIF (50 gp)") so you don't have to open a confirmation to see it. RIF auto-disables with explanatory tooltip when: the character is The Founder, is on a quest/grind, or is in downtime. Level Up uses the same ov-lvlup modal with its existing confirmation; after completion, the char modal auto-refreshes its header/body to reflect the new level, skills, tools, etc. RIF uses the same inline confirm flow as before but closes the char modal on success since the character no longer exists. All busy-state and Founder checks still run at click time as belt-and-braces.
Version 1.6898 — Party screen reorganization. (1) Individual character cards removed. All characters now appear inside squad roster panels (from v1.6896). Characters not in any squad appear in a new virtual "Unassigned" panel with a grey border and 🧭 icon, appended after real squads. Virtual = derived each render, not persisted in S.squads — so no lifecycle problems when you rename or rebuild squads. (2) XP / downtime detail modal (ov-xpbadge) z-index elevated to 510 so it layers above the character modal (ov-charpeek, default z:500). Previously the XP modal opened but was hidden behind charpeek due to DOM order. (3) Uninitiated XP tracks (0 XP) no longer each get a full button row in the Overview tab; instead they collapse to a single compact "Not yet started: ⚔ 🗣 🗺 ✨" footer with low-opacity icons.
