(2) Squad manager target icons moved from card corner to individual skill/tool/lang/faith tags — a 🎯 appears as a superscript on each specific attribute the unassigned character has that the focused squad currently lacks. (3) QP balance hidden when zero — updateQpDisplay() hides hdr-qp-side and divider when bal===0, same as when QP is disabled. (4) EXOTIC_TOOLS: Poisoner's Kit and Cobbler's Tools replaced with Cartographer's Tools and Navigator's Tools. (5) Downtime badges styled to match XP badges: same border, border-radius, padding, font-size, and font-family; teal color. (6) Downtime badge dialog matches XP format: progress bars per track, percentage fill, all earned badges listed at bottom.
Version 1.545 — (1) Squad manager target icons: when a squad is sorted/focused via the ribbon, unassigned characters who cover at least one attribute the squad lacks get a 🎯 icon in the top-right corner of their card. Hovering shows a tooltip listing the specific gap skills/tools/languages/faith. The icon only appears for unassigned characters (those in another squad are excluded). (2) Party screen "Available" filter button added to the action bar — filters to characters not currently on an active quest or grind mission, i.e. those who can use Downtime. Button toggles (active state shown in green); clears if clicked again. Uses __not_questing__ tag key via charHasTag.
Version 1.544 — Six fixes for character 33 test: (1) Store mobile — store-content gets min-height:0 and -webkit-overflow-scrolling:touch so all tabs scroll correctly on mobile. (2) Gear sub-tabs changed from 3 price-based (1–4/5–10/11+) to 4 themed categories: ⚔ Weapons, 🛡 Armor, 🎒 Gear, 🧰 Kits (includes ADVENTURING_GEAR items). (3) Quest-character-assignment carousel arrows repositioned: absolute-positioned on sides of the viewport at vertical center (top:50%/translateY); viewports get margin:0 30-32px to create space. (4) GP display given border-radius:20px to match the QP pill shape. (5) Building assign dropdown: already excluded for non-artifact assets by canAssignReward flag — no change needed. (6) Bag of Holding recognition fixed with _isBagOfHolding() fuzzy helper checking name.includes('bag')&&name.includes('holding') — handles custom-named variants like "Longinus' Carpetbag of Holding". Capstone reward stores item under its actual name in franchiseStorage. Both openSquadNew and sqSave use the helper.
Version 1.543 — Two fixes: (1) Task pie (pct) now persists: pct field added to active_tasks Supabase push row and restored on pull. Requires ALTER TABLE active_tasks ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS pct integer NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 (already run). (2) Capstone Bag of Holding persistence fixed: the bag was awarded to S.partyAssets in memory but the Supabase push may not have completed before the next pull overwrote it. Fix: Bag of Holding is now also added to S.franchiseStorage (string entry) as a durable fallback that travels in the franchise row. awardCapstonReward now calls save() and schedules an explicit sbPush(false) 200ms after the alert is dismissed to guarantee the bag reaches Supabase before any reload.
Version 1.542 — (1) Task pie (pct) now persists to Supabase: pct field added to active_tasks push row and restored in pull. Subtask pct values already travel in the subtasks JSONB column. A pct column is needed in Supabase (see SQL below). (2) Added top-level console.log calls at the entry of openSquadNew() logging partyAssets names/types, franchiseStorage contents, and the hasBag result to diagnose the Bag of Holding recognition issue.
Version 1.541 — (1) Store category tab bar: footer now starts at bottom:0 and is repositioned to nav.offsetHeight in renderStore() so it sits flush on top of the nav regardless of device/safe-area. Scroll spacer updated via requestAnimationFrame after footer renders. (2) franchiseHasBagOfHolding() enhanced with [BagCheck] console logs for each check path to diagnose why the squad creation modal may still show the wrong card. The logic is: storage → inventory → partyAssets (any bag not ownerType=squad).
Version 1.540 — (1) Dev bar now appears below the header, not over it. z-index lowered from 491 to 180 (below header's 220). updateDevBar() reads hdr.offsetHeight and sets bar.style.top dynamically so it always sits flush below the header regardless of its rendered height. (2) Bag of Holding recognition consolidated into franchiseHasBagOfHolding() helper that checks franchiseStorage (strings and objects), character inventories, and partyAssets (any bag whose meta.ownerType is not 'squad'). This correctly handles capstone-awarded bags with type:artifact and meta:{uid} but no ownerType. Both openSquadNew and sqSave now use this logic.
Version 1.539 — Four fixes rolled into store layout update: (1) Dev +1 Day now also auto-resolves all active downtime jobs — sets endsAt to now-1 on each job, calls runDowntimeCompletion(), clears downtimeJobs[], and logs how many were resolved. (2) Faith/religion added as a sixth ribbon in the wishlist (🙏 Faith), pulling from DND.rel; candidate cards already highlighted faith matches. (3) Quest-character-assignment peg slots now correctly preserved when hiring a hireling — the renderQuestAssignmentList wrapper no longer re-inits existing slot keys to null when extraChecked is present; only truly new slot keys are initialized. (4) Bag of Holding received as a capstone reward (type:artifact, no explicit meta.ownerType) is now recognized by the squad creation hasBag check and sqSave bag search — both now use a safe (a.meta&&a.meta.ownerType)||'' pattern instead of assetMeta(a).ownerType which could return undefined on artifact-typed assets.
Version 1.538 — Store layout rebuilt: category tabs now live in a fixed #store-footer positioned directly above the bottom nav (bottom: nav height + safe area). Sub-category drawers animate upward from the top of the tab bar using max-height transition on the same dark background. The content area (#store-content) scrolls independently with a dynamic spacer that adjusts to the footer height so items are never hidden behind the tabs. Active tabs show gold highlight; inactive tabs are muted on the dark bar.
Version 1.537 — Recruiting gold cost mismatch fixed. Root cause: when a max-gold filter was active in the wishlist, renderCandidates re-indexed the filtered results starting at 0, so data-hire="0" on a card pointed to index 0 of the filtered list. The hire handler used that index to look up recruitCandidates[0], which was a different (more expensive) character. Fixed by preserving original recruitCandidates indices when building the filtered display — the hire button now always refers to the correct candidate regardless of which wishlist filters are active.
Version 1.536 — Two bug fixes: (1) Recruit screen no longer traps the user. Root cause: RENDER.party was redirecting to recruit whenever recruitCandidates was non-empty — but candidates were never cleared on close, so navTo(party) always bounced back. Fixed with a _recruitInProgress flag: set when candidates are rolled, cleared on close button click and on successful hire. RENDER.party only redirects when the flag is set. (2) Store panel was displaying on every screen. Root cause: s-store had an inline style="display:flex" which overrode the .scr{display:none} CSS rule, making it permanently visible. Fixed by removing the inline style and adding #s-store.on{display:flex;flex-direction:column} to the stylesheet so it only becomes flex when active.
Version 1.535 — Database schema migration: sbPush and sbPull updated to use proper Supabase columns instead of JSON blobs. franchise table: qp_balance, qp_earned_today, qp_earn_date, franchise_storage, downtime_jobs, quest_log now stored as dedicated columns; settings blob is clean. party table: stats, xp, dt_progress, downtime_badges, coin_purse, is_contact, contact_bg, exotic_trait, former_hireling, owned_items, first_hire now in proper columns; inv array no longer contains __meta__ sentinel string. squads table: squad_name, emoji, color, member_names in proper columns; name column is clean squad name. quest_cycles table: set_id, cycle_num, grind_pending, repeat_counts, set_progress, quest_cycles_history, quest_cycle_squads in proper columns; assigned blob contains only explicit character assignments. All pulls include legacy blob fallbacks for old rows, so existing data is preserved through the migration automatically.
Version 1.534 — Three fixes: (1) hasAnyOwnedBuildingAsset now checks franchiseStorage — buildings purchased via quest modal and stored there unlock franchise storage. (2) Store sub-category drawers are a vertical stack (flex-direction:column) sliding from the right via max-width animation. (3) navTo(party) redirects to recruit screen if candidates were rolled (recruitCandidates or hirelCandidates non-empty).
Version 1.533 — Ten fixes: (1) Hole tap flashes compatible peg cards yellow. (2) Common language is free — always included, not counted against random slots. (3) Dev bar uses position:fixed; body.dev-active adds margin-top. (4) GP gold text / QP white on bright blue. (5) Store two-column layout — category tabs stacked left, sub-drawers in right column. (6) Buildings skip char/squad assignment UI. (7) Slot assignments preserved when hiring hireling from quest modal. (8) qddRefreshInfoPrereqs() called after render to keep ⚠ warnings current. (9) QP balance uses typeof check on save/load. (10) Dev bar mobile fix.
Version 1.532 — Nine features: (1) Dev stat block on party cards. (2) Wishlist scroll preserved on toggle. (3) Max gold ribbon (100–600 gp) filters candidates. (4) Recruit auto-redisplays on return. (5) GP gold text / QP white-on-blue. (6) Store two-column: tabs left, drawers right. (7) WotC species names injects native language (Elvish for Elves, etc.). (8) Quest nav scrolls to first assignable quest. (9) questPrereqHtml shows "✗ none available" in red for prereqs no unassigned squad member can cover.
Version 1.531 — Dev mode shows STR/DEX/CON/INT/WIS/CHA stat block on party screen character cards between inventory and action buttons. Six stats with label, value, and modifier. Only visible in dev mode.
Version 1.530 — Seven improvements: (1) Wishlist selected pills sort to front of ribbon; deselecting returns to natural position. (2) Store drawers inline below each tab (each drawer directly below its tab in DOM). (3) QP toggle: hdr-qp-side.hdr-sw-active gets bright blue background (#1a6fd4). (4) questReadiness uses active cycle squad members for checks. (5) Exit Dev in bar via flex-wrap fix on #dev-bar.active. (6) Level-up buttons removed from prereqs info card (live on peg cards only). (7) Buildings forced to franchise storage in buy flow; buildingReq checks both partyAssets and franchiseStorage.
Version 1.529 — Eight changes: (1) Login pills reset to 27–[email protected]. (2) Wishlist clear button added. (3) Wishlist scroll position preserved on deselect. (4) Store drawers inline below each tab. (5) Store search box searches all items across tabs. (6) Exit Dev inline in dev bar. (7) Exit Dev no longer asks for confirmation. (8) ⚡ Recall button on in-progress quests: spends 25 QP, removes from pending, releases characters, no gold reward.
Version 1.528 — Six improvements: (1) Wishlist expanded to Skills/Tools/Languages/Classes/Species ribbons with 🎯 target icons for missing party attributes. (2) Dev bar constrained to max-width:480px. (3) QP spam guard: tasks <10 chars or no vowel in first 5 chars skip QP vesting. (4) Bottom nav only hides on mobile keyboard (visualViewport shrink <75%), not desktop focus. (5) Dev bar Ledger button navigates to gold log screen. (6) Capstone reward bug fixed: _alreadyDone checked before pushing to done[], so reward now actually fires.
Version 1.527 — Five fixes: (1) Subtask pie recalculates on subtask add. (2) Wishlist ribbon added to recruit screen: sqmgr-style ribbons for Skills/Tools/Languages/Classes/Species. 🎯 target icon on attributes not in party. Candidate cards highlight matching attributes green. (3) Subtask checkbox pie updates in-place to reduce nav flicker. (4) Storage Shed found by store-link (BUILDINGS added to lookup; subtab auto-set to exp-buildings). (5) Peg card level-up clickable: qcb-lvup intercepted before data-peg-ci handler.
Version 1.526 — Level-up button added to quest character peg cards: shown when quest has explicit level requirement and character falls short. Uses existing qcb-lvup handler. Only for non-hirelings who are not busy.
Version 1.525 — Five improvements: (1) Hire ribbon applies to hirelings too (focusNewHireling scope fix). (2) Quest description card: line-clamp removed; vehicle/building requirements get 🛒 Purchase button opening expedition store tab. (3) Checking a subtask auto-increments parent task pie (done/total×6 formula). (4) Hireling level-up cost raised to 200 gp at all four cost sites. (5) Recruit candidate cards show roster count badges on skill/tool/lang/faith/class/species pills; zero counts highlighted green.
Version 1.524 — Four quest modal improvements: (1) Hire modal (3s modal → 2s ribbon, ⚔ Next Quest + 👤 View buttons). (2) GP/QP fully mutual — clicking GP hides it, shows QP only; clicking QP hides it, restores GP. Dev bar adds ⏩ +1 Day button (completes quests, +400 gp, +20 QP). (3) Unmet level requirement shows level-up buttons for squad members below requirement instead of Recruit link. (4) Item prerequisites in questPrereqCheck now only count items carried by characters in peg slots — storage no longer satisfies item prereqs.
Version 1.523 — Hire ribbon becomes a modal: centered modal with 🎉, character name, "⚔ Next Quest" and "👤 View [Name]" buttons. After 3s shrinks to a green ribbon for 2s then fades. Clicking overlay or either button dismisses immediately. Hire applies to hirelings from quest panel too. encumbranceRelinquishPrompt: instead of blocking a transfer, user can relinquish an equal-or-heavier item to franchise storage. Default quest duration set to 30s. speciesNames defaults to true. Store: Adventuring Gear on second row. Store search box added. Dev bar Exit Dev inline.
Version 1.522 — Six gold diagnostic [Gold] console.log statements added at every S.gold assignment point to help trace the gold reset bug.
Version 1.521 — Login pills updated to 20–[email protected] with tester1 password. Register button restored.
Version 1.520 — Login: test user dropdown replaced with pill buttons 20–[email protected]. Register button restored.
Version 1.519 — QP switch: GP only shown on launch. QP side revealed on first task creation with shooting-star animation (12 emoji stars fly from random positions toward the QP badge). revealQpWithStars() awards 20 QP and switches pill to QP highlighted. _qpRevealed flag prevents repeat. Login panel: dropdown of test users 1–10 auto-fills email+password. "+ New Test User" button creates next sequential account, adds to dropdown, auto-logs in.
Version 1.518 — Login credentials updated to [email protected] / tester6.
Version 1.517 — Login panel: email/password pre-filled with [email protected] / tester5.
Version 1.516 — QP enabled by default (cfg.qp.enabled=true). 20 QP welcome bonus awarded on fresh account creation. QP side of switch visible when enabled.
Version 1.515 — GP/QP pill replaced with a proper two-sided toggle switch: hdr-gp-side and hdr-qp-side as separate spans with hdr-sw-active class for highlighting. QP side hidden until revealed. Divider hidden when only one side shows.
Version 1.514 — GP/QP pill rebuilt again as flex track with proper overflow:hidden clipping. Both panels are 100% wide inside a 200%-wide track; sliding to QP uses translateX(-50%) to bring QP exactly into view.
Version 1.513 — Store tab order: Adventuring Gear first (default open with 1–4 gp sub-drawer), Magic second. storeTab defaults to "adventuring".
Version 1.512 — Two quest fixes: (1) Characters held busy until gold is claimed — pending entries hold characters regardless of whether the quest timer has expired; released only when the user collects the reward. (2) qddAssignToSlot eviction bug fixed — incoming character (ci) is removed from any other slot they occupied, but the previous occupant (prev) is no longer evicted from all their slots.
Version 1.511 — Level requirement error message improved: if a character on another quest would satisfy the level requirement, message now reads "Level N+ required — your highest eligible adventurer is on another quest. Wait for them to return." instead of generic "Party level N+ (highest: 0)".
Version 1.510 — GP/QP pill rebuilt as flex track (two 100%-wide panels side-by-side, clipped by overflow:hidden). Sliding to QP moves the track translateX(-50%). Clicking GP panel triggers switch to QP; clicking QP switches back.
Version 1.509 — QP switch introduced in header pill: GP and QP share a two-sided toggle. GP side shown by default (gold, active). QP side (⚡, blue) hidden until QP is enabled. Periodic auto-peek every 25–60s briefly shows QP then returns to GP. Tapping manually toggles. QP enabled by default. 20 QP awarded on first task creation with a shooting-star animation. _qpRevealed flag prevents repeat.
Version 1.508 — Pie checkbox interaction fix: subtask checkbox moved outside the label element so checking a subtask no longer simultaneously triggers the checkbox AND the pie toggle. Pie button intercepts click before the label.
Version 1.507 — Several polish changes: (1) Squad "Filter by" button added to the collapsible squad inventory bar on the party screen, between the Edit button and the squad icon. When that squad is the active filter, it reads "✕ Unfilter". Filtering by squad shows full character cards (not collapsed), with the squad tag rendered as a plain pill rather than the crimson active style. Top action bar gains "✕ Clear filter" when any filter is active. (2) "Sever" renamed to "RIF" with title="Reduction in Force" tooltip; confirmation dialog and log entries updated to use reduction-in-force language. (3) Exploration XP badge rank 3 changed from "Ranger" to "Woodsman". (4) Task and subtask progress pie: a 28px SVG circle to the left of each task and subtask title, divided into sixths. Starts white/empty; each tap fills the next sixth clockwise from 10 o'clock in a different color (amber, blue, green, red, purple, orange). Seven taps cycles back to empty. No border, no text — just the pie. Value stored as step 0–6 on t.pct / s.pct.
Version 1.506 — Several polish changes: (1) Squad filter buttons added to the collapsible squad inventory bar on the party screen — "Filter by" button between Edit and the squad icon; shows "✕ Unfilter" when that squad is already the active filter. When filtering by squad, full character card content is shown (skills, languages, inventory, tools — not collapsed), and the squad tag renders as a plain pill rather than the crimson active style. Top action bar shows "✕ Clear filter" when any filter is active. (2) "Sever" button on character cards renamed to "RIF" with title="Reduction in Force" tooltip. Confirmation dialog reads "Reduction in Force: release [Name] from the roster for X gp?"; gold log reads "RIF: [Name] released"; blocked-action alerts say "cannot be released" in all cases. Internal data-a="sever" action name unchanged. (3) Exploration XP badge rank 3 changed from "Ranger" to "Woodsman" (Scout → Pathfinder → Woodsman → Wayfarer → Master Explorer).
Version 1.505 — Three auto-assign improvements: (1) Candidate pool restricted to members of the active cycle's squad — characters from other squads can no longer be pulled in by auto-assign. Falls back to full party if no squad is assigned. (2) canAutoFill() simulation: dry-runs the full greedy covering algorithm (same squad restriction, same busy-character filter, adventurers then hirelings) without mutating state. Returns true only if all prereq slots can be covered by available squad members. (3) Auto button (⚡ 1 QP) is grayed out (disabled + opacity 0.4) whenever canAutoFill() returns false, with an updated tooltip. Re-enables automatically when conditions change (hireling hired, slot filled manually, etc.).
Version 1.504 — Quest-map Activate button: if a squad is already assigned to the cycle, skip the squad-picker modal and instead close the map, navigate to the Adventure screen, and scroll to the first unfinished quest (first assign button, falling back to first collect button) with a smooth scroll. Squad picker still opens normally when no squad is assigned yet.
Version 1.503 — Auto-assign algorithm rewritten. Old algorithm: greedy slot-by-slot, placed any available character into any slot regardless of whether they covered it, used hirelings. New algorithm: Phase 1 — find the minimum set of adventurers to cover all prereq slots using a set-cover greedy (pick the candidate covering the most remaining unfilled slots, assign them to all those slots, repeat until no adventurer covers any remaining slot or all slots filled). Phase 2 — fill support slots with remaining adventurers who meet the level requirement. Hirelings are excluded from auto-assign entirely (they require deliberate manual hire via the quest panel). Characters who cover no prereq slots (e.g. high-level generalists) are placed in support, not forced into prereq slots they cannot fill.
Version 1.502 — averageLevelForIndices now excludes hirelings (level 0) from the calculation. Hirelings are temporary workers, not adventurers, and their level-0 status was unfairly dragging down the party average used for cycle gate requirements (minAvgSquadLevel checks). A squad of four level-5 adventurers plus two hirelings now correctly reports an average level of 5, not 3.3.
Version 1.501 — Comprehensive squad system cleanup and quest-cycle integration: Squad inventory now appears for ALL squads on the party page (not just those with assets), showing member count and item count. Squad-owned assets are excluded from the Purchased Assets panel and Franchise Storage. Bag of Holding gets party:true so it routes through the asset-assignment modal when purchased. Enrollment adds bag directly to partyAssets (not franchiseStorage). Meta mutation set directly on asset object. cycleSquadMap() auto-cleans stale entries for disbanded squads on every call. Disband also explicitly removes cycle assignment. squadIsLockedByCycle() helper gates roster changes in sqmgrAssignChar, sqSave, and the squad edit modal — cycle-locked squads show a crimson notice with checkboxes disabled. Quest hire panel checks squad capacity and shows "Squad full" instead of Hire button when at 10; on hire, new hireling is auto-added to the active cycle squad if a spot exists. Stale squad badge removed from quest-map modal. Version numbering resumed at 1.501.
Version 1.500 — Over-selection behaviour changed to next-to-last-out at all three points: the newest selection always succeeds; it is the second-newest (the one immediately before it) that gets evicted. (1) Squad Manager: assigning to a full squad removes the most recently added existing member (splice at length-1), animates them sliding left, then adds the new character. (2) Squad creation checkboxes: checking an 11th box slides away the second-to-last checked box (allChecked[length-2]). (3) Mercenary toggles: tapping when at the limit evicts list[length-1] with a scale-fade animation, then adds the new selection.
Version 1.500 — Three party-screen and squad-modal fixes: (1) Squad inventory items on the party page no longer show a re-assignment dropdown — items show icon, name, and a "squad only" label. The only way to release a squad bag is to disband the squad. (2) The returning-customer enrollment button on the used-car-salesman card changed from green-on-green (btn bgn + background:forest) to gold-on-white (background:gold-dark, border:gold) so it is legible. (3) Squad filter/tag chips removed from the party page; replaced with a slim two-button bar (Manage Squads + New Squad). Edit buttons moved into the left side of each collapsible squad inventory summary bar, so squads with assets can be edited directly from their inventory header. Also: next-to-last-out eviction at all three selection-limit points (squad manager, creation modal, mercenary toggles).
Version 1.499 — Squad bag-of-holding assignment now uses the same mechanism as the store: when a new squad is created and a Bag of Holding exists in franchise storage, it is moved from S.franchiseStorage into S.partyAssets with meta {ownerType:'squad', ownerRef:squadName} — exactly as the buy modal does for squad-assigned items. Removed the previous bagStorageIdx approach. Party screen now shows collapsible squad inventory sections (coloured by squad colour, labelled by squad emoji + name) above the character list for any squad that has items assigned to it. Each item row shows the asset icon, name, and a re-assignment dropdown (same as the party assets panel) so players can move items between squads, characters, storage, or buildings from the party view.
Version 1.498 — Three squad improvements: (1) On squad creation, an unclaimed Bag of Holding from franchise storage is automatically assigned to the new squad via bagStorageIdx (only one squad per bag — first-come basis). (2) Assigning a character to a full squad (10 members) in the Squad Manager now silently evicts the longest-standing member (LIFO: shift from index 0) with a slide-left fade animation, then adds the new member. Same behaviour in the squad creation members list — checking an 11th box animates the first checked item sliding away before it is unchecked. No warning or redirect. (3) Mercenary hire skill/tool/language toggles: hitting the max no longer shows an alert. Instead, the oldest selection is silently evicted (list.shift()) with a scale-down fade animation on that button, then the new selection is added.
Version 1.497 — Three squad manager improvements: (1) 10-member limit now shows a smooth warning overlay ("Squads hold a maximum of 10 adventurers / Saving your progress…") that fades in over 1.4s then fades out before transitioning to the Squad Manager, instead of the abrupt immediate switch. (2) Character cards in the Squad Manager now show ALL of a character's skill proficiencies by default. When a ribbon filter is active, they instead show the relevant proficiency type (all skills with the filtered one highlighted, all languages, all tools+weapons, or faith) — so the filter context is always visible on the card. (3) If the franchise already owns a Bag of Holding, the enrollment modal shows an alternative card in the voice of a used-car salesman, offering squad re-registration for 50 gp ("skip the certified package, sew your own tabards…"). The standard 500 gp brochure is shown when no Bag of Holding exists anywhere in franchise storage or character inventories.
Version 1.496 — Squad modal redesigned as a 3-card horizontal swipe with dot indicators: (1) Card 0 — for new squads, shows an enrollment brochure listing branded livery, charter document, territory registration, bulletin board listing, ID badges, and a free Bag of Holding (400 gp value) for the 500 gp enrollment fee. Paying the fee awards the Bag of Holding to franchise storage and advances to card 1. For edits, card 0 shows the name field and a Next button. (2) Card 1 — identity: squad name, 16 emoji symbols, 10 colour swatches. (3) Card 2 — member selection with a running (N / 10) counter; hitting 11 saves progress and redirects to the Squad Manager. Save button on card 2 commits all changes. Disband button visible only in edit mode. openSquadNew() and openSquadEdit() replace inline modal setup code at all call sites.
Version 1.495 — Fixed squad emoji and color not persisting across devices. Root cause: sbPush only serialised squad name and member names into the Supabase squads table row; emoji and color were silently dropped. sbPull reconstructed squads without them, so every pull reset squads to default values. No SQL migration needed — fixed by packing emoji and color into the name string as a __sqmeta__{...} suffix (alongside the existing __members__ suffix). sbPull now strips and parses __sqmeta__ before __members__, restoring both fields. Old rows without __sqmeta__ fall back to defaults gracefully.
Version 1.494 — Squad manager improvements: (1) Unassigned tile added as the permanent 8th cell in the 4×2 squad grid (grid now supports up to 7 squads + always-present None tile). Selecting a character then tapping None calls sqmgrUnassignChar, removing them from all squads. Tapping None with no character selected sorts unassigned members to the top. (2) Single skill ribbon replaced with four filter ribbons (⭐ Skills, 🙏 Faith, 🗣 Lang, 🔧 Tools). Each is a horizontally scrollable row with a fixed label at left. Tools ribbon merges DND.weapons and DND.tools. Chips only appear for values at least one party member holds. Selecting any chip filters the character list; selecting the same chip again clears the filter. Only one ribbon filter is active at a time.
Version 1.493 — Fixed emoji and color pickers in squad modal being completely unresponsive. Root cause: btn-sqmgr-resort was removed from the overlay HTML during the v1.490 redesign but its addEventListener call remained in the script. Since script execution is sequential, the null-reference crash on that line prevented all subsequent event handler registrations from running — including the ov-sq-emojis and ov-sq-colors click handlers. Fixed by removing the dead addEventListener call. The remaining guarded reference (in sqmgrReSort flash) was already safe.
Version 1.492 — Fixed emoji and color pickers in the Add/Edit Squad modal being unresponsive. Root cause: SQUAD_COLORS was updated to desaturated values in v1.489 but the static HTML color buttons still had the old saturated hex codes. sqResetEmojiColor matched data-c values against the saved squad color, found no match, and left no button selected — making the picker appear stuck. Fixed: color picker HTML buttons updated to match SQUAD_COLORS exactly. Also added a fallback in sqResetEmojiColor so if no button matches the saved value, the first button is selected, preventing a permanently empty picker state.
Version 1.491 — Squad manager rebuilt around a peg-and-hole interaction model: (1) Fixed 4×2 squad tile grid at top — up to 8 squads, empty cells shown as dashed placeholders. Tapping a squad tile while no character is selected sorts that squad to the top of the list; tapping the same tile clears the sort. (2) Horizontally scrollable skill filter strip below the grid — one chip per D&D skill showing member count. Tapping a skill filters the character list to members with that proficiency. (3) Character cards show Name (bold), Class, Level, and their 2 best skills ranked by the governing ability score (e.g. Persuasion ranks by CHA, Athletics by STR) — using c.stats assigned at character creation/graduation. (4) Peg-and-hole assignment: tap a character card to select it (gold ring highlight, hint bar updates), then tap a squad tile to assign. Character immediately moves to that squad's color. SKILL_ABILITY mapping covers all 18 D&D skills to their governing ability.
Version 1.490 — Squad manager redesigned around a paint-bucket model: (1) Fixed 3×3 grid of squad tiles at the top — up to 8 squads + an "Unassigned" tile, empty placeholders fill remaining cells. Tiles show emoji, name, and a selected indicator (white ring). (2) Swipe system removed. Instead: tap a tile to select it as the active paint bucket, then tap any character row to assign them — the row flashes to the new color and the icon animates, then the list re-sorts after a 520ms beat to group same-squad members together. (3) Character zone is fully scrollable (flex:1 overflow-y:auto), grid is fixed above it. Hint bar between grid and chars updates to show the active bucket name. Filter system removed in favor of the paint interaction.
Version 1.489 — Squad manager overhaul: (1) Squad background colors desaturated for readability. (2) Squad creation member list now hides characters already assigned to other squads — only unassigned characters and the squad's current members appear. (3) Character rows enlarged: padding 10×12, emoji icon 1.3em, name/class/skill all .9em, name bold. (4) Squad cards reorganised into a 2-column Pinterest grid with emoji, name, and member count only — no character rows inside. (5) Squad cards are now filter toggles: tapping a card filters the character list below to show only that squad's members. An "Unassigned" card (with count) also appears in the grid. Tapping the active card clears the filter. Filter resets when the manager is opened.
Version 1.488 — Two fixes: (1) Quest character assignment: tapping ✕ on a filled peg-slot (hole-remove) now clears ALL slots that character occupies, matching the behavior of removing the character card from the carousel. Previously only the tapped slot was cleared, leaving the character silently filling other slots. (2) Squad manager redesigned as a flat list: all party members appear in one scrollable list with icon and background color indicating squad membership — no grouping headers. Characters with no squad show a neutral row. Swiping left cycles to the next squad (or back to unassigned after the last), swiping right goes the other direction. The legend at the top shows squad chips with name, emoji, member count, and an edit button. One-squad-per-character rule enforced: swipe removes from current squad before adding to the next.
Version 1.487 — Squad management overhaul: (1) Squad creation/edit modal now includes emoji selector (16 options: ⚔🛡🗡🏹🔥❄⚡🌿💀🐺🦅🌙☀⭐🎯🪄) and color picker (10 rich background colors). Emoji and color saved to squad object. (2) New Squad Manager overlay (⚔ Manage button on party screen): 2-column Pinterest-style grid of squad cards, each showing emoji, name, member count, and a compact single-row card per member (emoji · name · class · level · primary skill). (3) Swiping a member row left/right cycles the character through available squads with an animated emoji/color transition and re-renders on completion. (4) Re-sort button at top reorders S.party so squad members are grouped together, updating all memberIdxs to match the new indices.
Version 1.486 — Three quest modal fixes: (1) Auto-advance to page 2 of the peg-hole carousel now only triggers if all 4 non-support prerequisite slots on page 1 are filled — quests with fewer than 4 prereqs no longer trigger the slide. (2) Store sub-tab drawer max-height increased to 120px and buttons given min-width:0 plus smaller padding/font so all three Adventuring Gear tabs fit on narrow mobile screens without clipping. (3) Quest assignment modal footer buttons condensed: Cancel shows ✕ only, Auto shows ⚡ 1 QP, Begin shows ⚔ Begin — all with title attributes for accessibility. Each fits on one row at default accessibility font size.
Version 1.485 — Auto-assign button improvements: (1) Button is now greyed out (disabled) when non-character prerequisites are unmet (gold, items, level requirement) — character-slot prereqs are what auto-assign handles; the other blockers must be resolved manually first. Vehicle/building bonus rows are excluded from this check since they are not hard gates. (2) Animation slowed significantly: filled peg-slots each glow green in staggered sequence (180ms apart), then after all slots are lit the Begin Quest button pulses with a scale+glow animation (500ms), then fires after a further 350ms pause — giving the user time to see each step.
Version 1.484 — Quest assignment modal abandon behavior: Cancel button and clicking outside the modal now call questModalAbandon() which clears all peg-slot assignments and resets modal state before closing — so the modal always opens blank. Recruiting from the modal (via 🧑 Recruit button) does NOT clear assignments since the user is not abandoning, just stepping away briefly. Auto-assign (⚡ Auto) now calls qddAutoAssignAndSubmit(): if QP was spent, after filling slots it flashes the Begin Quest button (scale pulse + green glow) and then auto-presses it, so the full flow completes in one tap. In dev mode the button is free but does NOT auto-submit (intentional — dev users may want to inspect before beginning).
Version 1.483 — Fixed ⚡ Auto button not appearing in non-dev mode when QP is enabled. Root cause: qddRender was hiding the button whenever S.qpBalance < cost, so players with 0 QP never saw it existed. Fixed: button now always shows when QP is enabled (or in dev mode), and is visually disabled (opacity 0.45, disabled attribute) when balance is insufficient — tapping it shows an informative alert. Also clarified: the "auto-running on load" appearance is intentional — the modal restores previously assigned characters from S.questState.assigned on open, so a quest you previously auto-assigned will re-populate those characters. This is not the auto-assign firing; it is assignment memory.
Version 1.482 — Store and auto-assign revisions: (1) Magic tab sub-tabs reduced to two: Common (all common items + 5 randomly selected uncommon) and Rare (10 randomly selected rare items). buildStock() updated accordingly. (2) Adventuring Gear price ranges tightened to 1–4 gp / 5–10 gp / 11+ gp. (3) Auto-assign (⚡ Auto button on quest assignment modal) cost reduced from 5 QP to 1 QP. Auto-assign already implemented: fills prerequisite peg-slots with the minimum number of characters using a coverage-maximising greedy algorithm, spending 1 QP per use (free in dev mode).
Version 1.481 — Expanded magic item pool using SRD content. Common: added Potion of Climbing, Cantrip Spell Scroll, 1st-Level Spell Scroll, Potion of Animal Friendship (5 total, was 1). Uncommon: added 31 items including Alchemy Jug, Boots of Elvenkind, Broom of Flying, Cloak of Elvenkind, Gauntlets of Ogre Power, Hat of Disguise, Headband of Intellect, Helm of Comprehending Languages, Portable Hole, Ring of Jumping/Mind Shielding/Warmth/Water Walking, Slippers of Spider Climbing, spell scrolls (2nd and 3rd level), +1 Armor, Wand of Web, Wind Fan, and more (48 total, was 17). Rare: added 16 items including Boots of Levitation, Cube of Force, Folding Boat, Ioun Stone (Reserve), Mantle of Spell Resistance, Ring of Feather Falling/Free Action/Spell Storing, Robe of Eyes, Wand of Lightning Bolts/Paralysis, Wings of Flying, and more (29 total, was 13). Veryrare unchanged at 7 — kept in system but not shown in store.
Version 1.480 — Store restructured: (1) Magic divorced from Gear — now its own tab with Common/Uncommon/Rare sub-tabs; veryrare and legendary items exist in the system but are not shown in the store. Reroll applies only to the Magic tab. (2) Adventuring Gear merges the existing GEAR non-magic items (weapons, armor, kits, mundane gear) with the ADVENTURING_GEAR array, split into three price sub-tabs (<10 gp / 10–50 gp / 50+ gp). (3) Buildings merged into Expedition Assets under a two sub-tab system (Vehicles & Mounts / Buildings). (4) All tabs with sub-categories use a slide-out drawer animation (max-height transition) that opens on tab select and closes when another tab is chosen. (5) buildStock() now draws only from magic items by rarity.
Version 1.479 — Five UX improvements: (1) Party inventory: personal (owned) items now collapse into a <details> toggle "🔒 Personal (N)" — non-personal items still cluster by category. Reduces scrolling for characters with large inventories. (2) Quest screen cycle title enlarged (1.05em bold) and description enlarged (.88em) for better readability. (3) Content Editor cycle form now includes editable Name and Description/Theme fields — changes save alongside gate rules. (4) Party screen Assets/Stats/Recruit buttons given equal height (min-height:36px, flex stretch) for visual consistency. (5) Store restructured: Gear & Magic tab gains Daily Stock / Magic Items sub-tabs; Buildings merged into Expedition Assets with Vehicles & Mounts / Buildings sub-tabs; new Adventuring Gear tab with 30 mundane items in three cost sub-tabs (Under 10 gp / 10-50 gp / 50+ gp).
Version 1.478 — Changelog backfill: added full descriptive entries for versions 1.459 through 1.477. These versions had been recorded as header version bumps only, with no detail in the change log. All 19 entries now describe root causes and fixes.
Version 1.477 — Five UX improvements: (1) Quest description card now shows a level requirement row with a 🧑 Recruit button that navigates to the recruit screen and auto-rolls candidates if none exist. (2) Party screen inventory auto-clusters into Weapons / Armor / Packs & Kits / Magic / Other with category headers. (3) Quest screen sort selector removed — quests always display in published order. (4) Quest cards show a compact prereq summary line (skills, tools, items, gold, level) above the Assign & Begin button. (5) Reroll buttons on adventurer and hireling candidate lists moved to the bottom of each list.
Version 1.476 — Fixed quest completion history (e.g. Rising Reputation) not persisting across devices. S.questState.setProgress, S.questCycles, and S.questCycleSquads were never saved to Supabase — only in localStorage. On new devices, isSetCompleted() found no evidence of completed cycles, blocking prerequisite gates. Fixed: all three are now packed into the quest_cycles assigned blob on sbPush and unpacked on sbPull, and also into the franchise settings backup path.
Version 1.475 — QP balance badge moved inside #gold-disp to prevent header row wrapping on mobile. Previously a standalone sibling in the auto column, it pushed the settings gear to a new row. Now renders as an inline suffix within the gold display: '🪙 150 gp ⭐ 4 QP'.
Version 1.474 — Scope fix applied to all remaining editor save handlers. Grind, gear, and asset (vehicle/building/pet) saves now resolve targetScope from item._visibility rather than the scope dropdown. Delete handlers for grind and gear also updated. All six editor save paths (quest v1.464, cycle v1.473, grind/gear/assets v1.474) now correctly target the item's original stored scope regardless of the admin dropdown setting.
Version 1.473 — Fixed cycle gate rules (minAvgSquadLevel, minSquadSize, requiredCycleSetId, etc.) not persisting after save. Same root cause as the quest edit bug: getCustomQuestSets() read currentContentScope() (the dropdown, which defaults to 'official' for admins). Fixed: ec-save now resolves scope from set._visibility, reads/writes to the correct bucket, and pre-updates contentBucket before the async Supabase write.
Version 1.472 — QP Supabase sync. qpBalance, qpPending, qpLedger, qpEarnedToday, and qpEarnDate packed into franchise settings blob on sbPush and unpacked on sbPull — balance now persists across devices. Every qpAward and qpSpend also inserts to the qp_transactions table with an idempotency event_key (tid::type), preventing double-awards via the unique index. Supabase table required: see v1.471 notes.
Version 1.471 — QP (Quest Points) system foundation. Earn rates and spend costs stored in S.settings.qp with defaults: 2 QP per task created, 1 per subtask, 3 per group, 60-second vest delay, 50 QP/day cap. Earn hooks on task and subtask creation schedule a vest — QP only awarded if item still exists after the delay (prevents create-delete farming). QP Manager overlay (admin-only, ⭐ QP in dev bar) allows editing all rates, manual credit/debit testing, daily cap reset, and a live ledger view. Requires Supabase table: CREATE TABLE qp_transactions (id uuid PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(), user_id uuid, amount integer NOT NULL, balance_after integer NOT NULL, event_type text NOT NULL, event_key text, note text, created_at timestamptz DEFAULT now()); with unique index on (user_id, event_key).
Version 1.470 — Fixed squad assignment being silently cleared during quest/grind transitions. Root cause: v1.468 added abandonQuestCycle() which duplicated the existing openCycleAbandon modal flow and called delete cycleSquadMap()[setId] immediately on browser confirm(). Fixed: map card Abandon button now calls openCycleAbandon() (the proper modal with Cancel/Abandon). Duplicate function removed — one code path for squad removal.
Version 1.469 — Grind quest mechanics audit. Peg-hole carousel, item tags, roster rules, and XP confirmed consistent with main quests. One bug fixed: grind Begin gate was treating unmet skill/tool/lang prereqs as hard blockers (disabled Begin). Fixed: updateQuestModal grind path now separates roster failures (still block Begin) from missing prereqs (warn only, reward reduced). No-peg guard also added to grind path.
Version 1.468 — Quest map improvements: each cycle card now shows the assigned squad as a badge and quest progress count (e.g. '3/10 done') in the collapsed header — all concurrent squad assignments visible at a glance. Expanded cards with an assigned squad show an Abandon button (opens ov-cycle-abandon modal) which preserves completed quest progress, warns about in-progress quests, clears pending, and unassigns the squad.
Version 1.467 — Fixed cycle gate not recognising dev-completed prerequisite cycles. isSetCompleted() only checked setProgress (stale after switching cycles) and the questCycles archive (written only at calendar resets). Added direct checks: (1) against qs.done when the set was the active cycle, (2) against sp.done.length for any setProgress entry. Dev-completed cycles now satisfy gate requirements immediately.
Version 1.466 — Two fixes: (1) Card stack no longer causes horizontal scroll — qcs-viewport changed to overflow:hidden, peek card offsets reduced. (2) Begin Quest now requires at least one peg placed — previously qddSyncCheckboxes checked all boxes when no slots were assigned, so getQuestChars() returned the full party and the no-peg guard never fired. Fixed by unchecking all boxes when hasAnyAssigned is false.
Version 1.465 — Fixed quest edits not persisting when duplicate rows exist across scopes. An earlier bad save wrote the quest into both official and private buckets. mergeCustomContent concatenated official+shared+private and the official copy shadowed the private edit. Fixed: allQuests is now deduplicated by setId::idx before merging, with private > shared > official priority, so a user's private edit always wins.
Version 1.464 — Fixed quest edits saving to wrong Supabase scope. Console logging revealed scope=official when the quest lived in private. For admin users the scope dropdown defaults to 'official', so edFormScope() targeted the wrong bucket. Fixed: targetScope now reads q._visibility for existing non-builtin quests. The dropdown is only used for new quests.
Version 1.463 — Fixed quest edits being immediately reversed after save. Root cause: saveCustom for _customQuests first saves quest-set metadata (calling mergeCustomContent internally). At that moment contentBucket.quests still held old data, so QUESTSETS rebuilt with pre-edit values and edShowQuestForm re-rendered from them. Fixed: contentBucket(scope).quests is pre-updated before the chained save begins.
Version 1.462 — Fixed duplicate quest cycles not persisting after page reload. The Duplicate Cycle handler saved quest rows (content_type='quest') to Supabase but never saved the cycle metadata row (content_type='quest_set'). On reload, quests were orphaned with an unknown setId. Fixed: dupe cycle now calls persistContentScope for both '_customQuestSets' and '_customQuests' in sequence.
Version 1.461 — Dev bar Ledger button second-press now collapses the changelog <details> element and returns to the adventure screen via navTo('adventure').
Version 1.460 — Ledger button first press: navigates to Settings and opens the changelog. Second press: closes changelog and navigates back to adventure screen.
Version 1.459 — Fixed dev-bar Ledger button: was toggling the quest log panel (adventure screen) instead of the change log. Ledger now calls navTo('settings') and opens the changelog <details> element.
Version 1.458 — Dev mode quick-access bar: when developer mode is active a sticky navy bar appears below the header with four controls: Ledger (toggles the quest log panel), Editor (opens the content editor), version number label, and Exit Dev Mode (confirms and disables dev mode). The bar is hidden in normal play and shown/hidden via updateDevBar() which is called by renderSettings() and the dev mode toggle.
Version 1.457 — Description card prereq list: (1) skill, tool, language, and faith rows removed — these are already represented by the peg-slot holes below; (2) the "covered"/"have it" status notes removed for a cleaner look, only vehicle/building bonus notes (+reward) are kept; (3) when more than 3 prereq rows remain, the list switches to a two-column CSS grid layout; (4) unmet item prerequisites show a small shop button (🛒) — tapping it opens the buy modal for that item directly from the quest screen, and after purchase the quest modal automatically re-opens with the description card updated to show the item as satisfied.
Version 1.456 — Fixed duplicate capstone rewards on page reload. Root cause: autoCollectExpired() fires twice on startup — once from local data, then again after sbPull returns. The first pass collected the capstone and removed it from pending. But the sbPull restore logic then put localPending back (because the pulled state had fewer entries), re-adding the collected quest. The second autoCollectExpired fired awardCapstonReward again. Two fixes: (1) the localPending restore now filters out any quest indices already in qs.done so already-collected quests are never re-added to pending; (2) collectQuest now checks qs.done before calling awardCapstonReward as a secondary guard.
Version 1.455 — Custom content now visible in store for all users. Root cause: loadContentLibrary() is asynchronous but renderStore() was not called after it completed, so custom gear and pets from Supabase only appeared after a manual navigation. Fixed by calling renderStore() and renderAdv() at the end of loadContentLibrary() success path. Also cleared the gear stock date cache (S.stockDate) when content loads, so custom gear items can appear in that day's stock immediately rather than waiting until the next day's reroll.
Version 1.455 — Content editor scope fix: the editor was defaulting to Private scope, so all saved gear/pets/vehicles/buildings/quests were only visible to the saving account. The Supabase RLS SELECT policy was correct (allows shared+official with status=published) but nothing was being saved as shared. Fixed: editor now defaults to Shared when opened (Official for admins). Non-admin users can no longer accidentally select Official (downgraded to Shared instead of Private). To fix existing rows in Supabase, run: UPDATE content_items SET visibility = 'shared' WHERE visibility = 'private' AND status = 'published' AND content_type IN ('gear','pet','vehicle','building'); Add quest_set and quest to the IN list if those should also be shared.
Version 1.454 — Content editor persistence fixed. Root cause: CONTENT_LIB was a standalone global never saved to localStorage, so all custom content (gear, pets, vehicles, buildings, quest cycles, quests) was lost on every page reload regardless of Supabase status. Fixes: (1) saveLocalState() now also writes CONTENT_LIB to localStorage under key rq_content:{uid}. (2) load() restores CONTENT_LIB from that key before mergeCustomContent runs, so custom content is available immediately on startup and for guests. (3) loadContentLibrary() guest path no longer wipes CONTENT_LIB (it was overwriting the just-restored local data). (4) After a successful Supabase pull, the fresh CONTENT_LIB is cached to localStorage so it is available offline. (5) persistContentScope() now has a .catch() that surfaces Supabase errors in the sync status bar instead of silently swallowing them. Note: if content still fails to save to Supabase, check the content_type_check constraint on the content_items table allows all 7 types: quest_set, quest, grind, gear, vehicle, building, pet.
Version 1.453 — Auto-advance animation: when all page-1 slots are filled the carousel now signals the transition clearly. After a 180ms pause: the next-arrow pulses with a scale bounce (qhole-page-nudge keyframe), the viewport border flashes gold (qhole-vp-flash keyframe), and the slide itself uses a springy overshoot easing (cubic-bezier .34,1.22,.64,1) instead of the normal ease-in-out. The springy transition is applied only for the auto-advance and restored to the default immediately after.
Version 1.452 — Hole carousel auto-advances to page 2 when all four slots on page 1 are filled. Triggers inside qddAssignToSlot after both explicit assignment and auto-fill propagation complete, so it fires whether the user dragged a peg, tapped a hole, or a hireling was auto-placed. Support slots (always filled) count toward the "page full" check, so quests with fewer than four character prereqs advance as soon as the actual prereq slots are all covered.
Version 1.451 — Fixed hole carousel page 2 rendering blank. Root cause: qholeCarApply used translateX(-100%) to advance one page, but CSS translateX percentages are relative to the element's own width. With a 2-page track (width:200% of viewport), translateX(-100%) moved the track 200% of the viewport — twice as far as needed, sending page 2 completely off-screen. Fixed by computing pageWidthPct = 100/pages and using translateX(-(offset * pageWidthPct)%) so each page step moves exactly one viewport-width.
Version 1.450 — Quest modal peg cards now show item prerequisite coverage tags. Previously, a character carrying a Longsword would satisfy "Martial Weapons" once assigned to a slot (gate check used inventoryHasPrereqItem correctly) but showed no match indicator on their card in the carousel, because item slots are not hole slots. Fixed: qddRenderPeg now checks the quest's item prereqs against each character's inventory and displays a green bag tag (e.g. "Martial Weapons") when covered. Characters covering item prereqs also sort higher in the carousel.
Version 1.449 — Content editor polish: (1) clicking "+ New" on the Quests tab now defaults the quest set selector to whichever cycle you were most recently browsing, instead of always QUESTSETS[0]; (2) clicking "+ New" on Assets tab remembers the last-selected category (vehicle/building/pet) instead of always defaulting to vehicle; (3) removed a duplicate "var canEditBuiltin" declaration in the grind form editor that could cause unexpected behaviour in strict mode.
Version 1.448 — Data consistency audit: cross-checked every quest/grind prerequisite (skills, tools, languages, faith) against the DND character generation arrays used by the hire/level-up UI and the content editor dropdowns. Found and fixed one mismatch: four quest prereqs used "Gloriosus, the Braggart" while DND.rel defines "Gloriosus, the Bold". Affected quests: "Negotiate the Truce", "Drive Off the River Scavengers", and related content, plus a prefab NPC. All now use the canonical name. Martial Weapons in quest prereqs is correctly an item prereq (party must own them), not a skill — no change needed.
Version 1.447 — Quest assignment modal UX improvements: (1) Skill/tool/language/faith prerequisites now shown in the description card info rows alongside gold/item prereqs, with live party coverage check and a Hire button for each unmet req. (2) Unmet skill/tool holes in the slot carousel now show "tap to hire" and open the hire panel directly when tapped without a character selected — no need to locate the recruit peg card. (3) Hire button delegation moved from hidden #ov-q-prereqs to the modal container so buttons from both the card stack and prereq panel work. (4) Char carousel counter shows "No adventurers" instead of "1-0 of 0" when party list is empty.
Version 1.446 — Fixed "Begin Quest" grayed out for rare/perilous quests even when all prerequisites are met. Root cause: questPrereqCheck derived a minimum level from the quest tier (rare=5, perilous=7, etc.) and used it as a hard gate. Tier minLv was always meant as a readiness colour hint (green/yellow/red on the card), not a blocker. Level now only hard-gates if the quest has an explicit minLevel or minLv field set directly on it. Dive the Outer Colonnade (rare) and similar quests are now beginnable by any party meeting their skill/tool/item/gold prerequisites.
Version 1.445 — Quest hire panel: (1) panel repositioned to appear inline just above the slot carousel instead of hidden at top of scroll, so it is immediately visible when a recruit card is tapped; scrollIntoView called on open and scroll resets to top of modal; (2) after hiring, the new hireling is automatically assigned to the prerequisite slot they were hired for — animate-to-hole fires if the peg is visible in the carousel, otherwise assignment is made directly; slot key is initialized if not already present to prevent silent no-ops.
Version 1.444 — Critical runtime fix: six state variables (qddSlotAssignments, qddQuestLike, qddIsGrind, qcarOffset, qcarEntries, qcarSelected) and two functions (qddCurrentQuestLike, qddSlotNeedsHireling) were missing from the quest assignment modal code block after a previous session deleted a stale duplicate block. This caused a ReferenceError on every quest modal open. Also fixed: squadName in confirmQuestAssignment now uses activeCycleSquadName() so terrain/mount checks work correctly; three more quest array lookups (autoCollectExpired, qcollect handler, nonCapstoneDone) now use questByIdx().
Version 1.443 — Dev mode quest controls: when dev mode is active each quest card shows a labelled row with context-sensitive buttons: “Complete” instantly marks a not-started quest done and awards baseGp to the franchise (triggering capstone rewards if applicable), “Revert” removes a completed quest from the done list and reverses the recorded gold and character pay from the log entry, “Cancel” aborts an in-progress quest and releases characters without awarding gold. All operations save state, re-render the quest screen, and write a log entry.
Version 1.442 — Content editor: capstone quests now support a custom reward override. Choose between a specific item/artifact (name, icon, description), a specific pet (name, icon, description), or a custom NPC contact (full character spec: species, background, class/role, skills, tools, languages, faith, starting inventory, exotic trait/note). If no override is set the existing random pool is used. NPC contacts awarded this way are added to the party roster as contacts with full character data.
Version 1.441 — Fixed “Assign & Begin” button on the quest screen: the click handler looked up quests via set.quests[idx] using the quest’s idx value as a direct array position. After recent mergeCustomContent refactoring (which rebuilds and re-sorts the quests array from multiple sources), array position no longer reliably matches idx. Added questByIdx(set, idx) helper — fast-path check then linear scan — and applied it at all five lookup sites: qassign click handler, confirmQuestAssignment, updateQuestModal (two references), and the modal duration label.
Version 1.440 — Content editor persistence fixes: (1) ☁ Push and ⬇ Pull buttons added directly to the editor header so Supabase sync is accessible without closing the editor. (2) Quest save no longer reads stale legacy S.settings._customQuestSets — always uses live contentBucket data. (3) Asset category changes now chain save promises (remove old category, then save new) instead of racing. (4) Form scope selector (ef-scope-sel) now syncs back to the header scope selector (ed-content-scope) on change so they stay consistent.
Version 1.440 — Fixed Begin Quest button remaining disabled when franchise assets (e.g. Diving Bell) satisfied all item prerequisites: prereq checks, roster checks, and hard-gate checks now all use the same resolved roster (squad members or full party). Fixed duplicate function definitions causing stale logic to override corrected code. Hole carousel page 2 now renders correctly (CSS flex override removed). Quest map modal is collapsible and scrollable with a ✕ close button.
Version 1.438 — Quest assignment modal: moved title above info card stack; fixed franchise assets (e.g. Diving Bell) counting toward item prerequisites; peg tags show only personal proficiencies; hired hirelings auto-animate into their slot; compatible holes persist a gentle glow while a card is selected; quest list always fully expanded unless the cycle is complete (grind mode); removed quest-screen collapse toggle from normal play.
Version 1.437 — Redesigned quest character-assignment modal: sliding info card stack (quest/details/best-fit) with visible peek-behind cards; prerequisite holes in a 3-column grid; character mini-cards in a swipeable carousel sorted by prereq coverage; tap to select a card, tap a flashing hole to animate it into place; support slot only accepts characters already filling a prereq; hireling recruit cards surface automatically for uncoverable requirements; drag mechanic removed; cancel and begin share one button row.
Version 1.436 — Rolled back quest-assignment-level squad enforcement and moved squad requirement to Quest Map cycle activation: activating a cycle now requires picking an available squad (one squad can only be assigned to one cycle at a time), and if no squads are available the flow prompts squad creation with non-busy members preselected.
Version 1.435 — Added collapse/expand controls for quest cycles in the Content Editor list; surfaced orphaned custom quests (missing set links) so they can be found again; renamed “Severe” action label to “Sever”; and ensured sell menu values never drop below 1 gp for expanded inventory items.
Version 1.434 — Fixed hireling graduation so Level 1 graduates count as full adventurers for quest prerequisites; improved Content Editor save reliability/visibility; added capstone artifact assignment controls on Assets; refreshed exotic language pool to remove overlaps with now-standard language options.
Version 1.433 — Reworked Content Editor CSV import/export to handle quest rows only (header = quest properties), while Cycle Rules and Gear remain managed in their existing in-app editor forms.
Version 1.432 — Added CSV import/export workflow for Content Editor data so quest cycles and gear groups can be edited in Google Sheets-style tab rows, including cycle-rules rows, buffer rows, and quest-row bulk import.
Version 1.431 — Prevented automatic viewport jumps during asset transfer/update actions, added character-name quick-view links (outside Party/Recruitment) to open a character card modal, and made Stats coverage rows clickable with Party filter behavior.
Version 1.430 — Added bulk purchasing quantity for acquisitions, expanded assignment targets (building/vehicle/squad) for assets and supply caches, collapsed mount/pet naming controls by default on Party Assets, and updated mountain support gating to count Mules assigned to the selected squad.
Version 1.429 — Unified prerequisite picker lists with character capability lists (skills, tools, languages, and religions) so Content Editor prerequisites always match what characters can actually have; includes Calligrapher’s Supplies availability for Hasufan prerequisites.
Version 1.428 — Expanded hireling first-name pool and enforced rerolls to avoid duplicate first names with existing rostered hirelings. Added a Severe roster action on character cards (cost: 25 gp per level, including level 0).
Version 1.427 — Quest prerequisites now align with Content Editor fields by adding editable Prereq Items, and martial/simple weapon category prerequisites continue to match qualifying inventory weapons.
Version 1.426 — Updated item prerequisite matching to use D&D 2014 weapon groups: Simple Weapons and Martial Weapons now correctly match qualifying weapons in inventory (for example, Longsword satisfies Martial Weapons requirements).
Version 1.425 — Switching cycles from the Quest Map now preserves per-cycle progress instead of resetting. Cycle-completion prerequisites are now managed in Cycle Rules (set-level) rather than quest-level fields, and quest-level cycle prerequisite enforcement has been removed from assignment flow.
Version 1.424 — Content Editor quest form now includes optional Vehicle Support and Building Support fields, so set-level support requirements like The Rival’s Warehouse needing a Storage Shed are directly visible and editable from the editor.
Version 1.423 — Fixed a stray Settings markup leak at the bottom of non-Settings screens, kept the top header fixed while scrolling, disabled repeat quest clears, and added cycle prerequisite support for pet counts (for example, any 3 pets).
Version 1.422 — Expanded Quest Map and cycle-level editor wiring with visibility modes and cycle-level prerequisites.
Version 1.421 — Added the Tier 2 “Hasufan in the Eastern Juxians” quest cycle and mountain logistics gating.
Version 1.420 — Added quest cycle map controls plus cycle/squad/terrain/difficulty assignment gating support.
Version 1.419 — Added map button and multi-stage quest progression updates.
🧪 Test Mode
Test mode only — changes quest timer for new assignments. Use the fast-forward button below to instantly complete quests already in progress.
🎲 Dev Rolling Table
Two-second icosahedron roll simulation with high-speed entry, edge bounces, and settling before result.
Press Roll 1d20.
🎲 Recruitment Names
When enabled, recruits receive names typical for their species. First names are never reused.
✏️ Content Editor
Add and edit quests, grind missions, and gear. Custom content merges with built-ins and syncs through Supabase cloud save. Tab between fields, Enter to save.
Data
Import replaces all current data. Export first to back up.
🧪 Milestone Loader
Loads a curated test snapshot. Archive groups and settings are preserved; active party, gold, contracts, quest progress, storage, and logs are replaced.
🧪 Developer Tools
Enable dev mode to access quest test tools, content editor, milestone loader, rolling table, and data tools. Dev mode is per-session and resets on logout.
☁ Cloud Sync (Supabase)
Auto-syncs after every change. Pull to load from another device.
✏️ CONTENT EDITOR ✓ Saved

🕐 Back Burner

Defer "" with a quick option or choose a specific date and time.

Defaults to the nearest upcoming 3-hour block.

🕯 Downtime Assignment

Choose a downtime activity for "" and set when it should complete.

8h is standard. Shorter sessions incur a roll penalty; longer sessions add a bonus.

Rolling...

🕯 Pending Downtime Rolls

⚠ Forfeit Downtime Progress?

Assigning this roster to a quest will cancel active downtime immediately. Any uncollected downtime progress/rewards will be forfeited.

⚠ Limited Contact Assigned

One or more contacts on this quest have a limited number of quests remaining before departing the franchise. Each contact below will be used once if you proceed.

🕐 Defer Group

Defer all tasks in "" until a set time.

Relentless Quest Certified Operations Division
Enrollment Package Includes:
🪙 Branded livery & matching tabards
📜 Certified charter document (notarised)
🗺 Regional territory registration
📣 Guild bulletin board listing
🪪 Official member identification badges
🎁 FREE: Bag of Holding (400 gp value!)
Enrollment Fee
500 gp
Non-refundable. Groups cannot be certified without payment.
Current balance: 0 gp
🤝 Hey, buddy! Welcome back!
Look, I'm not supposed to say this, but... you already got the Bag of Holding, right? Still sitting in storage? Still perfectly good?

Between you and me — skip the whole certified package. Sew your own tabards. Print your own charter. I've seen franchises built on nothing but a handshake and a dream.

Today only: guild registration, territory filing, and we look the other way on the livery — all for just 50 gp. That's practically free.
✓ You already have a Bag of Holding in storage

Slots
Your Party
⚔

Squads Required

Your franchise needs a standing squad

To activate a quest cycle, you need to assign a Squad — a named group of adventurers who will tackle that cycle together.

Squads let you manage multiple active cycles at once, track which characters are deployed where, and unlock territory-based mechanics like the Territory Swap.

Tap Create Squad to build your first squad from your current roster. You can always rename, recolor, and adjust members later from the Party screen.

⚔ No Available Squad

All squads are currently assigned. Create a new squad from available adventurers to continue.

🎒 Acquire Item

This will count toward relevant quest prerequisites.

⭐ Badge

⚠ Encumbrance — Drop an Item

🕯 Continue Downtime

⚔ Assign to Squad

📦 Manage Item

🗺 Quest Map

Tap a cycle to expand · Activate to begin its quests

⚔ Assign Squad to Cycle

Choose a squad to activate this cycle.

🏳 Abandon Quest Cycle

⚙ Clock In to Grind Missions

Choose a squad to send to the grind shift. Multiple squads can be on shift simultaneously. A squad cannot be on both a quest cycle and grinds.

⚙ Grind Missions

Repeatable franchise jobs — common reward, no expertise gained

⚠ Back to HQ — Active Engagements

🏘 Real Estate

🏘 Name Your Property

📂 Move to Group

👋 Welcome to Your Franchise

Start by creating or activating contracts on the Contracts screen. Tap + Add to make a new task, then use the gold button on a task whenever you complete progress on it.

Visit the Recruitment Office from the Party screen to hire full adventurers or budget hirelings. Your Founder begins the company, but extra hands help you tackle larger jobs.

On the Quest screen, choose a quest, assign a valid party, and begin the adventure. Regular quests require at least one Level 1+ full adventurer. When they return, use franchise gold or each hero's purse to buy gear from the Store.

Tip: archived contracts can stay in storage for occasional work, and favourites can still repopulate automatically at your reset time.

>
